summary: Juno has two coexisting native auth systems. (1) LEGACY (what the shipped Swift app, build <=30, actually uses today): the app drives the Auth.js v5 cookie flow directly (GET /api/auth/csrf -> form-encoded POST /api/auth/callback/credentials -> persist authjs.session-token cookie), or opens {base}/app-auth with NO query params in ASWebAuthenticationSession and receives the raw session JWT via juno://auth?token=... (LegacyAppAuthHandoff). (2) NEW v1 NATIVE DEVICE-SESSION contract (what the Windows client must implement): a PKCE-S256 browser handoff. The client generates state/nonce/codeVerifier (base64url, 43-256 chars) plus a stable installationId, opens {base}/app-auth?state=&nonce=&code_challenge=&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback&installation_id=... in the system browser; the server (after web sign-in, incl. Google) issues a single-use 2-minute authorization code and client-side JS redirects to {redirect_uri}?code=&state=&nonce=. The client exchanges it at POST /api/v1/auth/token (JSON, camelCase) for a 10-minute HS256 bearer access JWT (aud "juno-native") + a rotating 30-day refresh token, creating a named NativeDeviceSession. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh rotates the refresh token (same familyId, sliding 30-day expiry); reuse of a consumed/revoked refresh token revokes the whole device session and token family (401 token_reuse_detected). Bearer-authed endpoints: GET /api/v1/auth/session, GET /api/v1/auth/devices, DELETE /api/v1/auth/devices/{id}, POST /api/v1/auth/logout, plus the rest of /api/v1 (bootstrap, changes, changes/stream, mutations, models). All /api/v1 responses carry X-Juno-Request-Id and X-Juno-Contract-Version: 1.0.0 and use a uniform error envelope {error:{code,message,requestId,retryable,retryAfterMs}}. Redirect URIs are a fixed allowlist of two custom schemes (no loopback), so the Windows Tauri client must register the com.liammagnier.juno:// URI scheme (or legacy juno://). Server code: /Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/lib/native-auth-core.ts, native-auth.ts, api-v1.ts, src/app/app-auth/page.tsx + handoff.tsx, src/app/api/v1/auth/**. The Swift AuthSession.swift/WebAuthService.swift implement only the legacy cookie flow — do not copy them for Windows.
contracts: BROWSER AUTHORIZATION (PKCE start): GET {base}/app-auth with query params (snake_case, from src/app/app-auth/page.tsx): state, nonce, code_challenge, code_challenge_method (must be literally "S256"), redirect_uri, installation_id. Validation (isValidBrowserAuthorization, native-auth-core.ts): state/nonce/code_challenge must match /^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{43,256}$/ (base64url, >=43 chars); redirect_uri must be EXACTLY "com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback" (NATIVE_REDIRECT_URI) or "juno://auth/callback" (LEGACY_NATIVE_REDIRECT_URI) — no other value, no loopback; installation_id must match /^[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{16,200}$/. If the web user is not signed in, server redirects to /sign-in?callbackUrl=<urlencoded /app-auth?...> and returns to /app-auth after sign-in (any web method incl. Google works).

AUTHORIZATION CODE DELIVERY: the /app-auth page (AppAuthHandoff, handoff.tsx) immediately does window.location.replace(`${redirectUri}?${new URLSearchParams({code, state, nonce})}`) and also renders an 'Open Juno' <a href> fallback button. So the deep link is: com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback?code=<...>&state=<...>&nonce=<...>. The code is a 32-byte base64url secret (randomSecret()), stored hashed (sha256 base64url), single-use (usedAt), TTL = NATIVE_AUTH_CODE_TTL_MS = 2 minutes (2*60*1000), bound to codeChallenge + redirectUri + sha256("installation\0"+installationId). Server does NOT verify state/nonce at exchange — the CLIENT must verify returned state (and nonce) equal the values it generated.

TOKEN EXCHANGE: POST {base}/api/v1/auth/token, Content-Type application/json, body (zod .strict() — unknown keys => 400): { "code": string(1..512), "codeVerifier": string(43..256, base64url charset enforced), "redirectUri": string(<=200, must equal the one used in the browser step), "installationId": string(16..200, must equal browser step), "deviceName": string(trimmed 1..120), "platform": string(trimmed 1..40), "appVersion": string(trimmed 1..40) }. PKCE check: stored codeChallenge must equal base64url(sha256(codeVerifier)) — i.e. challenge = SHA-256 digest base64url-encoded, method S256 only. 200 response: { "tokenType": "Bearer", "accessToken": <JWT>, "accessTokenExpiresAt": <ISO8601>, "refreshToken": <base64url, 48 bytes -> 64 chars>, "refreshTokenExpiresAt": <ISO8601>, "deviceSession": { "id", "name", "createdAt" (ISO) } }. Any failure (expired/used code, redirect/installation mismatch, PKCE mismatch, banned user) => 400 { error: { code: "invalid_grant", message: "The authorization grant is invalid.", ... } }.

ACCESS TOKEN: HS256 JWT (jose), header {alg:"HS256",typ:"JWT"}; claims: sub=userId, sid=deviceSessionId, sv=sessionVersion (int), typ="native_access", iss=origin of NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL, aud="juno-native" (NATIVE_ACCESS_AUDIENCE), iat, exp = iat + 600s (NATIVE_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS = 10*60). Signing key = sha256("juno-native-access-v1\0" + AUTH_SECRET). Client treats it as opaque; expiry is given as accessTokenExpiresAt ISO string in every token/refresh response.

REFRESH: POST {base}/api/v1/auth/refresh, JSON body strict: { "refreshToken": string(32..512) }. 200 response: { "tokenType": "Bearer", "accessToken", "accessTokenExpiresAt", "refreshToken" (NEW token — old one is consumed), "refreshTokenExpiresAt" (NEW sliding 30-day window: NATIVE_REFRESH_TTL_MS = 30*24*60*60*1000) } — note: NO deviceSession field here. Rotation semantics (rotateNativeRefreshToken, native-auth.ts): token is single-use; a child token with same familyId and parentTokenId is created; deviceSession.lastSeenAt updated. Presenting an already-used or revoked token revokes the ENTIRE device session (revocationReason "refresh_token_reuse") and all unrevoked family tokens, then returns 401 { error: { code: "token_reuse_detected", message: "Refresh-token reuse revoked this device session." } }. Concurrent-rotation race is treated as reuse (also revokes + 401 token_reuse_detected). Expired token / revoked session / banned user / unknown token => 400 invalid_grant "The refresh grant is invalid.".

BEARER AUTH (all /api/v1/* endpoints): header Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>, matched by /^Bearer ([^\s]+)$/. Failure modes (authenticateNativeBearer): missing/invalid header or invalid signature/issuer/audience => 401 code "unauthenticated" ("The access token is invalid." / "A bearer token is required."); expired JWT => 401 code "token_expired" ("The access token expired.") — this is the refresh trigger; session missing/revoked or userId mismatch => 401 code "device_revoked" ("This device session is no longer active."); user banned or user.sessionVersion != JWT sv claim (bumped by password reset, /api/auth/reset-password) => 401 "unauthenticated" ("This account session is no longer active."). Successful auth fire-and-forgets a lastSeenAt update.

SESSION INFO: GET /api/v1/auth/session (Bearer) => 200 { "profile": { "id", "name", "email", "image" }, "deviceSession": { "id", "name", "platform", "appVersion", "createdAt" (ISO), "lastSeenAt" (ISO) }, "accessTokenExpiresAt" (ISO), "contractVersion": "1.0.0", "minimumSupportedAppVersion": "3.0.0" }.

DEVICES: GET /api/v1/auth/devices (Bearer) => 200 { "devices": [ { "id", "name", "platform", "appVersion", "createdAt" (ISO), "lastSeenAt" (ISO), "revokedAt" (ISO | null), "current": boolean } ] } — ALL sessions for the user incl. revoked ones, ordered lastSeenAt desc; current = matches the calling device session. DELETE /api/v1/auth/devices/{id} (Bearer) => 200 { "revoked": true, "deviceSessionId": "<id>" }; revokes session (reason "user_revoked") + all its refresh tokens; already-revoked or foreign id => 404 code "not_found" ("The device session was not found."). Self-revocation via this endpoint is allowed.

LOGOUT: POST /api/v1/auth/logout (Bearer, no body) => 200 { "revoked": true }; revokes the calling device session with reason "logout" and all its refresh tokens. Access token keeps verifying cryptographically but bearer auth then returns 401 device_revoked.

ERROR ENVELOPE + HEADERS (src/lib/api-v1.ts): every /api/v1 response (success and error) carries headers X-Juno-Request-Id: req_<uuid> and X-Juno-Contract-Version: 1.0.0 (CONTRACT_VERSION). Errors: { "error": { "code": string, "message": string, "requestId": "req_...", "retryable": boolean, "retryAfterMs": null, "details"?: object } }. NativeAuthError codes (native-auth.ts): "invalid_request", "invalid_grant", "unauthenticated", "token_expired", "device_revoked", "token_reuse_detected", "not_found" — all retryable:false. Zod body failure => 400 "invalid_request" "The request body is invalid.". Unknown errors => 500 "server_unavailable" retryable:true.

LEGACY FLOW (what AuthSession.swift/WebAuthService.swift in juno-app actually do; do NOT build the Windows client on this): (a) /app-auth opened with NO query params returns LegacyAppAuthHandoff which deep-links juno://auth?token=<raw Auth.js session-token cookie value>; app adopts it as the cookie. (b) Direct credentials: GET /api/auth/csrf => {"csrfToken"} + csrf cookie; POST /api/auth/callback/credentials form-encoded (csrfToken, email, password, json=true) => Set-Cookie authjs.session-token (HTTP) / __Secure-authjs.session-token (HTTPS), JWT maxAge 30 days; validate via GET /api/auth/session => {"user":{id,name,email,image},"expires"}; sign-out via form POST /api/auth/signout. Legacy cookie endpoints return 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"} (different envelope than /api/v1).
behaviors: Sign-in UX: generate and persist a stable installationId once per install (16-200 chars of [A-Za-z0-9._:-]); per attempt generate state, nonce, codeVerifier as 32-byte base64url random strings (43 chars each — randomSecret()); compute code_challenge = base64url(SHA256(codeVerifier)); open the system browser at {base}/app-auth?state=...&nonce=...&code_challenge=...&code_challenge_method=S256&redirect_uri=com.liammagnier.juno%3A%2F%2Fauth%2Fcallback&installation_id=...; wait for the OS deep link; verify returned state (and nonce) match; POST the exchange within 2 minutes (code TTL) — single attempt only, the code is single-use even on network failure of the response.

Token lifecycle: access token lives 10 minutes; schedule proactive refresh (e.g. at ~8-9 min or on 401 token_expired). Refresh token lives 30 days but every refresh issues a replacement and a new 30-day expiry (sliding), so an app used at least monthly never re-prompts. ALWAYS persist the new refreshToken atomically before discarding the old one; never retry a refresh with the old token after receiving a response — a second use triggers family-wide revocation.

401 handling matrix: code "token_expired" => call /api/v1/auth/refresh, then retry the original request once; "device_revoked", "token_reuse_detected", "unauthenticated" => wipe local tokens and return to signed-out state (re-run browser flow); refresh returning 400 "invalid_grant" => also full sign-out. The access token JWT is bound to the issuing origin (iss = deployment URL) and AUTH_SECRET, so switching backend environments (prod/preview/local, as the Swift BackendConfiguration allows) invalidates stored tokens — key stored tokens by backend base URL.

Device identity: the client names its own session at exchange time — Swift-equivalent values for Windows would be deviceName = user-visible machine name (1-120 chars), platform = e.g. "windows" (1-40), appVersion = app semver (1-40). These appear verbatim in the devices list on every client. lastSeenAt updates automatically on every authenticated call and every refresh.

Devices screen behavior: list from GET /api/v1/auth/devices shows all sessions including revoked (revokedAt != null) with a "current" flag; revoke any device with DELETE /api/v1/auth/devices/{id}; sign-out of the current device uses POST /api/v1/auth/logout then clears local tokens regardless of network outcome (Swift signOut clears locally even if server call fails — mirror that best-effort semantic).

Session restore on launch (mirrors Swift AuthSession.restore, adapted to v1): load persisted refresh/access tokens from OS credential store (Swift uses Keychain; Windows should use DPAPI/Windows Credential Manager via Tauri), validate by calling GET /api/v1/auth/session (refreshing first if the access token is expired); on 401 clear and show signed-out; on network error keep tokens but present as signed-out/offline until confirmed (do not delete tokens just because the network failed).

Browser handoff page behaviors to expect: if the web user is not signed in, they land on /sign-in with callbackUrl back to /app-auth — Google OAuth works here (this is the whole point of the browser handoff; the legacy direct-credentials flow cannot do Google). The page auto-fires the deep link via window.location.replace AND shows a manual 'Open Juno' button; the Windows client must tolerate receiving the same deep link twice (dedupe by state).

The Swift app also pushes personalization settings to the server once after each explicit sign-in (SettingsStore.pushPersonalizationNow) — replicate whatever equivalent post-sign-in sync the Windows app needs after adopting a session.

Validation strictness to replicate client-side: all /api/v1 auth bodies are zod .strict() — sending any extra JSON field yields 400 invalid_request; codeVerifier under 43 chars is rejected both by zod (min 43) and isValidCodeVerifier.
reuse: /Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/lib/native-auth-core.ts — the constants and pure helpers port almost 1:1 to TypeScript in the Tauri renderer: NATIVE_REDIRECT_URI, NATIVE_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS (600), NATIVE_AUTH_CODE_TTL_MS (120000), NATIVE_REFRESH_TTL_MS, randomSecret (crypto.getRandomValues + base64url), pkceChallenge (SHA-256 -> base64url via WebCrypto), isValidCodeVerifier regex. Strip: jose JWT sign/verify, node:crypto imports (server-only; the client never verifies the JWT).

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/tests/native-auth-core.test.ts — port the PKCE/validation test cases as contract tests for the Windows client's PKCE helper (challenge determinism, base64url entropy rules, S256-only).

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/lib/api-v1.ts — copy the error-envelope shape (ApiV1Error fields code/status/message/retryable/details, CONTRACT_VERSION "1.0.0", header names X-Juno-Request-Id / X-Juno-Contract-Version) into the client's typed API layer.

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/Juno/Services/Backend/AuthSession.swift — reuse the STATE MACHINE design, not the code: Phase enum (signedOut/restoring/signingIn/signedIn), restore-on-launch that tolerates offline without dropping credentials, best-effort server logout, lastError surface. Strip everything cookie/CSRF-related — that is the legacy contract.

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/Juno/Services/Backend/BackendConfiguration.swift — reuse the environment-switching model (production/preview/local/mock with persisted editable URLs, production default https://chat.liams.dev, local default http://localhost:3000, preview default https://juno-preview.vercel.app) for a Windows settings pane; strip the cookie-name computed properties (sessionCookieName/csrfCookieName) and voiceRelayURL specifics.

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/Juno/Services/Backend/WebAuthService.swift — reuse only the flow shape (open browser -> await callback -> adopt session, cancel/no-token error taxonomy). In Tauri replace ASWebAuthenticationSession with shell.open + tauri-plugin-deep-link listener + tauri-plugin-single-instance forwarding.

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/app/app-auth/handoff.tsx + page.tsx — read-only reference for exactly which query params the deep link carries (code, state, nonce) and the not-signed-in redirect; nothing to port.
risks: Redirect URI is a hard allowlist of exactly two values — "com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback" and legacy "juno://auth/callback" (native-auth-core.ts NATIVE_REDIRECT_URIS). There is NO loopback/http://127.0.0.1 option, so the Windows client MUST register a custom URI scheme in the Windows registry (Tauri 2: tauri-plugin-deep-link with "deep-link" config for com.liammagnier.juno, plus tauri-plugin-single-instance so a second invocation forwards the URL to the running instance). Use the com.liammagnier.juno scheme; "juno" is reserved by the legacy Mac lineage and also delivers legacy token deep links (juno://auth?token=...).

The authorization code is minted during server-side render of /app-auth and expires in 2 minutes, single-use. If the browser tab sits on the handoff page (user distracted, browser blocked the app-open prompt) the exchange fails with invalid_grant and the whole browser round-trip must restart. Also note: nothing stops the page being refreshed — a refresh issues a NEW code, and the old one stays valid until TTL; dedupe deep links by state.

installationId and redirectUri are bound at code-issue time and re-checked at exchange (hashed comparison). Using a fresh installationId per attempt works, but a STABLE per-install id is the intent (indexed userId+installationIdHash) — changing it per sign-in creates unlinked device sessions.

Refresh rotation is strictly single-use with family-wide revocation on reuse, and a lost response is unrecoverable: if the client sends a refresh, the server rotates, and the response is dropped by the network, the client only holds the consumed token — the next refresh attempt triggers token_reuse_detected and kills the device session. Persist-before-use ordering and a single-flight refresh mutex are mandatory; never issue parallel refreshes (the race path is deliberately treated as reuse and revokes the session).

Access-token issuer = origin of NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL and the signing key derives from AUTH_SECRET; tokens are deployment-specific. Preview deployments with a different AUTH_SECRET/origin will 401 "unauthenticated" (not token_expired), which the client must map to sign-out, not refresh-loop.

Password reset (/api/auth/reset-password) increments user.sessionVersion; existing access tokens then fail bearer auth with generic 401 "unauthenticated" even though the JWT is unexpired, and device sessions are NOT revoked in the DB — but the refresh flow does not check sessionVersion, so a refresh mints a new access token with the NEW sessionVersion and the session keeps working. Do not assume 'unauthenticated' always means dead session: try one refresh before wiping (or accept the sign-out; both are defensible — Swift app has no precedent here).

juno-app/docs/BACKEND_API.md documents ONLY the legacy cookie contract (it even points at an outdated source path /Users/liammagnier/Desktop/Liam) and predates /api/v1; treat src/app/api/v1/** and src/lib/native-auth*.ts as the sole source of truth for the Windows client. The legacy endpoints use a different error shape ({"error":"Unauthorized"}) and form-encoded bodies.

GET /api/v1/auth/session advertises minimumSupportedAppVersion: "3.0.0" (hardcoded) and contractVersion "1.0.0" — the Windows client should send an appVersion >= 3.0.0-style semver at token exchange and honor a future version gate.

Query params on /app-auth are snake_case (code_challenge, redirect_uri, installation_id) while all /api/v1 JSON bodies/responses are camelCase (codeVerifier, redirectUri, installationId) — easy to mix up.
Server never validates state/nonce at exchange; CSRF/linking protection of the deep link is entirely the client's job (reject callbacks whose state does not match an in-flight attempt).
Store refresh tokens in OS-protected storage (Swift uses Keychain via KeychainService); on Windows use Credential Manager/DPAPI (e.g. tauri keyring), never localStorage in the WebView.

Revoked device sessions remain in the GET /api/v1/auth/devices list (revokedAt set) — the UI must filter or badge them; DELETE on an already-revoked device returns 404 not_found, not idempotent success.

summary: The TypeScript agent core (/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/core/src, package @juno/agent-core) is a small, cleanly layered agent runtime: AgentSession (agent.ts) runs a per-turn loop (max 60 provider steps) that streams from a ProviderAdapter, dispatches tool calls sequentially through a PermissionEngine gate (modes plan/ask/auto-edit/full, deterministic sensitive-command regexes that ALWAYS require confirmation), snapshots mutated files per turn into a CheckpointStore for undo/rewind/diff, and persists everything in ~/.juno/sessions/<id>/ (meta.json, messages.json, events.jsonl). Surfaces attach via AgentCallbacks (onEvent + async requestApproval) or via the NDJSON-over-WebSocket sidecar (server.ts) that the Mac app uses. Providers: AnthropicAdapter (@anthropic-ai/sdk), OpenAICompatAdapter (openai SDK; zhipu + openai configured), and a backend-proxy mode (providers/proxy.ts) that points either SDK at the website's authenticated pass-through proxy POST /api/agent/<provider>/<path> with the user's session Cookie header — the SDK appends v1/messages or chat/completions and the backend injects the real key and streams back verbatim. Backend-billed turns are metered via BackendUsageReporter POSTing {phase:start|record|refund} to /api/agent/usage. types.ts + providers/types.ts + tools/types.ts define the entire client contract (AgentEvent union, PermissionMode, RiskLevel, ApprovalDecision, ChatMessage) with zero Node dependencies; agent.ts is ~90% portable orchestration with a handful of fs touches; tools, checkpoints, session store, and the bash tool are the Node-bound parts a Tauri app must reimplement in Rust. The CLI (apps/cli/src/index.ts) is a thin readline client over the same core and doubles as `juno serve <port>` sidecar host.
contracts: AgentEvent union (core/src/types.ts, copy verbatim — this IS the UI event stream): {type:'session_started', sessionId, cwd, provider, model, mode} | {type:'turn_started', turnIndex} | {type:'assistant_delta', text} | {type:'assistant_message', text} | {type:'tool_started', callId, name, input, risk} | {type:'tool_finished', callId, name, output, isError, durationMs} | {type:'tool_denied', callId, name, reason} | {type:'approval_requested', request:{callId, toolName, input, risk, summary}} | {type:'approval_resolved', callId, decision} | {type:'files_changed', turnIndex, paths:string[]} | {type:'mode_changed', mode} | {type:'turn_finished', turnIndex, stopReason, usage:{inputTokens, outputTokens}} | {type:'error', message}

Enums: PermissionMode = 'plan'|'ask'|'auto-edit'|'full'; RiskLevel = 'safe'|'edit'|'command'|'sensitive'; ApprovalDecision = 'allow'|'allow_always'|'deny'; turn stopReason values seen: 'end_turn'|'tool_use'|'max_tokens'|'other' (from providers) plus agent-level 'quota' (usage reservation refused) and 'error'

ChatMessage transcript format (messages.json, provider-neutral): user content blocks {type:'text', text} | {type:'tool_result', toolCallId, content, isError?}; assistant content blocks {type:'text', text} | {type:'tool_call', id, name, input}

Sidecar WebSocket protocol (core/src/server.ts), JSON messages, connect ws://127.0.0.1:<port>?token=<secret> (timing-safe compare, close code 4001 'invalid token' on mismatch). client->server: {type:'configure_backend', baseUrl, cookie, models:BackendCatalogModel[]} | {type:'start', cwd, provider?, model?, mode?} | {type:'resume', sessionId, mode?} | {type:'list_models'} | {type:'prompt', text} | {type:'approval', callId, decision} | {type:'set_mode', mode} | {type:'undo'} | {type:'diff', sinceTurn?} | {type:'list_sessions'} | {type:'delete_session', sessionId} | {type:'rename_session', sessionId, title} | {type:'abort'}. server->client: {type:'event', event:AgentEvent} | {type:'models', providers:ProviderListing[]} | {type:'diff', patch} | {type:'undo_result', restored:string[]} | {type:'sessions', sessions:SessionMeta[]} | {type:'protocol_error', message}

BackendCatalogModel (providers/proxy.ts, supplied by the surface in configure_backend): {provider (backend id, path segment under /api/agent, e.g. 'zhipu'), providerName?, kind:'anthropic'|'openai', model, label, available:boolean, reason?, vision?, contextWindow?}. Proxy provider ids are prefixed: BACKEND_PROVIDER_PREFIX = 'backend/' (e.g. 'backend/anthropic')

Backend LLM proxy (juno/src/app/api/agent/[...path]/route.ts): POST /api/agent/<provider>/<path> with the session Cookie header; body is the provider-native request forwarded verbatim. Allowed paths ONLY: 'v1/messages' for kind anthropic, 'chat/completions' for any openai-kind provider, 'responses' for provider 'openai' only — everything else 403 {error:'Endpoint not allowed.'}. Server injects key: anthropic → x-api-key + anthropic-version (client's header or default '2023-06-01') + forwards anthropic-beta; openai-kind → authorization: Bearer <key>. Errors: 401 {error:'Unauthorized'}, 429 {error:'Rate limit exceeded. Try again shortly.'} (120 req/60s per user, skipped for OWNER plan), 402 {error, code:'QUOTA_EXCEEDED'} on budget exceeded, 400 unknown provider, 502 provider not configured/unreachable. Response streamed back untouched, content-type preserved, cache-control: no-store. proxy.ts constructs AnthropicAdapter with baseURL=`<baseUrl>/<provider>` (SDK appends /v1/messages) or OpenAICompatAdapter with baseUrl same (SDK appends /chat/completions); apiKey placeholder is the literal string 'proxy'; the Cookie rides in defaultHeaders {Cookie: config.cookie}

Usage accounting (core/src/usage.ts -> juno/src/app/api/agent/usage/route.ts): POST <baseUrl>/usage (baseUrl e.g. https://chat.liams.dev/api/agent) with headers {content-type:'application/json', Cookie:<session cookie>}. Bodies: {phase:'start'} → 200 {ok:true, quota} or 402 {error, code:'QUOTA_EXCEEDED', quota?}; {phase:'record', model, promptTokens, completionTokens} → 200 {ok:true} (field names are promptTokens/completionTokens, NOT inputTokens); {phase:'refund'} → 200 {ok:true, quota}. Client fail-open policy: only HTTP 402 blocks a turn; 401/5xx/network failure must allow the turn to proceed

ProviderAdapter interface (providers/types.ts): {id, name, defaultModel, models():string[], capabilities(model):ModelCapabilities, stream(req):AsyncGenerator<ProviderStreamEvent>}. ProviderRequest: {model, system, messages, tools:ToolSpec[], maxTokens? (default 8192), signal?}. ProviderStreamEvent: {type:'text_delta', text} | {type:'thinking_delta', text} | {type:'tool_call', id, name, input} | {type:'done', stopReason:'end_turn'|'tool_use'|'max_tokens'|'other', usage}. ModelCapabilities: {tools, vision, computerUse, reasoningLevels:string[], maxContext, streaming, mcp}
ProviderListing (model picker payload, providers/registry.ts): {id, name, available, reason?, defaultModel, models:[{id, label, capabilities, available?, reason?}]}

Tool specs sent to the model (tools/fs.ts, tools/bash.ts): read_file {path, offset? (1-based line), limit?}; write_file {path, content}; edit_file {path, old_string, new_string, replace_all?}; glob {pattern}; grep {pattern (JS regex), glob?}; bash {command, timeout_ms? (default 120000, max 600000)}. ToolDefinition: {spec, kind:'read'|'edit'|'command', execute(input, ctx:{cwd}):Promise<{output, isError?}>, mutatedPaths?(input, ctx):string[], summarize(input):string}

SessionMeta (types.ts / meta.json): {id (format 'YYYY-MM-DD-<8hex>'), title, cwd, provider, model, mode, createdAt, updatedAt, turnCount}. Storage layout: $JUNO_HOME (default ~/.juno)/sessions/<id>/{meta.json, messages.json, events.jsonl (each line = {at:ISO, ...AgentEvent}), checkpoints/{index.json, <turnIndex>-<n>-<basename> snapshot files}}. Credentials file: ~/.juno/credentials.json = {"anthropic":{"apiKey":"..."}, "zhipu":{...}}; env vars win (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ZHIPU_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY)

Checkpoint index.json entry: {turnIndex, createdAt, files: Record<absolutePath, snapshotFilename|null>} — null means the file did not exist pre-turn (undo deletes it). Project permission rules file: <cwd>/.juno/settings.json = {allow:string[], deny:string[]} (tool names)
behaviors: Turn loop (agent.ts prompt()): push user message → emit turn_started → (if backend-billed) usageReporter.reserve(); a 402 refusal emits error + turn_finished with stopReason 'quota' and zero usage, before any model call. Then up to MAX_STEPS_PER_TURN=60 iterations: stream provider events (assistant_delta forwarded live via callbacks.onEvent but NOT persisted to events.jsonl; the final assistant_message IS persisted), collect tool_calls, accumulate usage across steps; break when stopReason !== 'tool_use' or no tool calls; otherwise execute every tool call SEQUENTIALLY in received order, then push all results as ONE user message of tool_result blocks and loop again. Messages are saved to disk after every step.

Tool gating per call: classifyRisk (kind read→safe, edit→edit, bash command matched against 14 sensitive regexes→sensitive, else command) then PermissionEngine.decide precedence: project deny-list → deny; sensitive → ALWAYS 'ask' (no mode, no allowlist, no allow_always bypass); plan mode → safe allowed, everything else denied; safe → allow; project allow-list or in-session grantAlways set → allow; then by mode: ask→ask, auto-edit→(edit allowed, command asks), full→allow. 'allow_always' decisions persist only in-memory for the session and only when risk !== 'sensitive'.

Approval flow: emit approval_requested (summary = tool.summarize(input), with ' — SENSITIVE (<reason>)' suffix when sensitive) → await callbacks.requestApproval (turn blocks) → emit approval_resolved. Deny returns tool_result 'The user declined this action.' with isError:true so the model adapts. Sidecar maps approvals by callId in a pendingApprovals Map; on socket close all pending approvals resolve 'deny' and the session aborts.

Checkpoints/undo: before executing a tool, every path from tool.mutatedPaths() is snapshotted under the current turnIndex (first write per turn wins; nonexistent file recorded as null). End of turn emits files_changed if anything was snapshotted. undoLastTurn() restores to before the LAST turn that has changes; rewindToTurn(n)/restoreToBefore uses the EARLIEST snapshot per file across all turns >= n, deletes null-snapshot files, and truncates the checkpoint index. diffSinceTurn produces unified diffs (npm 'diff' createTwoFilesPatch, a/<rel> b/<rel> headers) or 'No file changes.'. Bash-driven mutations are NOT checkpointed (documented limitation).

Usage reconciliation at turn end: if any tokens were consumed → record(model, usage) (errors swallowed); if zero tokens (provider error/abort before output) → refund(). BYOK (non-backend) sessions have no usageReporter at all.

System prompt rebuilt every step: identifies as 'Juno', includes cwd, platform, date; injects the first existing memory file in priority order JUNO.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md (capped 20,000 chars); plan mode appends a read-only-plan instruction and the tool list sent to the model is filtered to kind==='read'.

Output truncation rules: tool_finished event output capped at 2000 chars + '…' (full output still goes to the model); bash combined stdout+stderr capped 30,000 chars, non-zero exit appends '[exit code N]' with isError, timeout kills with SIGKILL and appends '[command timed out after Nms]'; read_file 50,000 chars; glob 200 results; grep 100 matches, skips files >2MB and binary (NUL byte); glob/grep ignore node_modules/.git/dist/build/.next.

Session lifecycle: title auto-set to first 60 chars of the first prompt; resume reattaches with the provider recorded in meta.json (server.ts resume passes cwd:'' — the stored meta.cwd wins); abort() cancels the in-flight provider stream via AbortController; sidecar rejects a second concurrent prompt with protocol_error 'a turn is already running' and refuses delete of a running session ('cannot delete a running session'); rename trims title to 200 chars and syncs the in-memory session so a later saveMeta doesn't clobber it; after configure_backend the server pushes a fresh {type:'models'} list unprompted; when backend models exist the proxy catalog fully REPLACES local BYOK listings (BYOK duplicates hidden).

Model catalog behavior: providers listed with available:false + reason ('Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or add it in Settings.') when no key resolves; key resolution order explicit arg → env var → ~/.juno/credentials.json; defaultProviderId() = first available provider; OpenAI provider specifically sends max_completion_tokens instead of max_tokens (all other openai-compat labs get max_tokens); openai-compat streaming accumulates tool-call argument fragments per index and JSON.parses at the end (unparseable args become {}); thinking_delta events are yielded by both adapters (anthropic thinking_delta, openai-compat reasoning_content) but agent.ts silently ignores them — a new client could surface them.

CLI behaviors worth mirroring: headless mode (-p) auto-denies every approval and prints 'auto-denied (headless): <summary>' to stderr, streams assistant text to stdout, tool traffic to stderr, exit code 1 on error events; interactive approval keys y/a/n where 'a' (always) is refused for sensitive; slash commands /mode /plan /undo /diff /sessions map 1:1 to core methods; sensitive command patterns (permissions.ts): rm -rf, sudo, git push --force/-f, git reset --hard, git clean -f, chmod 777, mkfs|diskutil erase|dd if=, shutdown|reboot|halt, killall, curl|wget piped to sh, paths touching .ssh/.aws/.gnupg, .env read/exfil combos, security find/dump-keychain, redirect to /dev/sd|disk|rdisk.

risks: Cookie header is a forbidden header in browser/webview fetch — the proxy provider and usage reporter both authenticate via a raw Cookie header (providers/proxy.ts headers={Cookie}, usage.ts). In Tauri the webview's native fetch will silently drop it; all backend-proxied traffic must go through @tauri-apps/plugin-http fetch (Rust reqwest, arbitrary headers allowed) or a Rust command. The Anthropic/OpenAI SDKs accept a custom fetch implementation, so pass the plugin fetch in.

Anthropic SDK requires dangerouslyAllowBrowser:true when instantiated in a webview (it throws otherwise); same flag for openai SDK. Versions in core/package.json: @anthropic-ai/sdk ^0.107.0, openai ^6.45.0, diff ^7.0.0, fast-glob ^3.3.2, ws ^8.18.0.

bash tool hardcodes spawn('/bin/bash', ['-c', command]) with env=process.env — does not exist on Windows. The Rust reimplementation must pick a shell (PowerShell/cmd/Git Bash) AND the sensitive-command regexes in permissions.ts are Unix-shaped (sudo, rm -rf, /dev/sd, keychain); Windows-equivalent patterns (Remove-Item -Recurse -Force, format, reg delete, etc.) need adding or dangerous commands will classify as plain 'command' risk.

The backend proxy allows ONLY POST to v1/messages | chat/completions | responses(openai only); the Anthropic SDK occasionally calls other endpoints (e.g. token counting) — any such call gets a 403 JSON body, not an Anthropic-shaped error. Keep max_tokens explicit (agent uses 8192) and avoid SDK features that touch other paths.

Usage metering fail-open is deliberate and asymmetric: ONLY HTTP 402 blocks a turn; 401/5xx/network errors must let the turn proceed (usage.ts comments are explicit). Replicating stricter behavior would lock paying users out on backend blips; replicating looser behavior (ignoring 402) would bypass billing.

Quota flow ordering: reserve() runs BEFORE the first model call and consumeMessage burns one message even if the user aborts instantly — the refund() path (zero tokens) is what returns it. A Windows client that skips refund on abort silently burns quota.

assistant_delta events are not persisted to events.jsonl (emitted via callbacks.onEvent only, agent.ts line 208); a client rebuilding history from events.jsonl or messages.json gets only final assistant_message text. thinking_delta from providers is dropped entirely by the current loop.

Session storage has no locking: sidecar + CLI + a new Windows engine writing the same $JUNO_HOME will stomp meta.json/messages.json. Windows profile default becomes %USERPROFILE%\.juno — fine, but honor the JUNO_HOME override for tests.
grantAlways ('allow_always') is per-session in-memory only, never written to .juno/settings.json — do not persist it in the Windows app or behavior diverges from Mac/CLI.

Checkpoints only cover write_file/edit_file (mutatedPaths); bash-driven file mutations are invisible to undo/diff — the UI must not advertise undo as covering shell changes. Snapshot restore uses whole-file copies; binary files work but diffSince reads them as utf8.

Sidecar security model: localhost-only WS + optional ?token= query param (timing-safe compared). If the Windows app adopts the sidecar architecture, generate a per-launch random token and never bind non-loopback; the token rides in the URL, so keep it out of logs.

The proxy provider catalog (BackendCatalogModel[]) is pushed by the surface via configure_backend — the Swift app fetches it from the website (models endpoint outside this subsystem, see juno/ backend mapping); the Windows client needs the same catalog fetch plus the session cookie, obtained from website auth.

core is ESM ('type':'module', NodeNext resolution) with .js extension imports — consuming source files directly in Vite works, but importing the built dist/ via the package export pulls in ws/fast-glob/node:fs transitively through index.ts; import the individual source modules (types, permissions, proxy transforms) rather than the barrel, or split the package into core-pure and core-node entry points.

Anthropic default model ids in the local (non-proxy) catalog: 'claude-sonnet-5' (default), 'claude-opus-4-8', 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'; zhipu 'glm-5.2'/'glm-5-air'; openai 'gpt-5.5'/'gpt-5.4-mini'/'gpt-5.3-codex'. In backend-proxy mode the website catalog is authoritative and replaces these.

summary: Juno has TWO documented design languages that a Windows client must reconcile. (1) The web app (/juno) is the "warm editorial" system: warm-paper light / warm-charcoal dark HSL tokens in src/app/globals.css, coral accent hsl(15 54% 51%), Newsreader serif as the ENTIRE UI typeface (font-sans and font-serif both resolve to it), JetBrains Mono for metadata/eyebrows, 24px card radius, film grain overlay, and a 'depth kit' of composed shadow/sheen utilities; floating chrome uses a frosted warm-glass recipe (bg-popover/80 + backdrop-blur-xl + .glass-raised). /juno/design.md is the definitive prose spec (note: its dark-theme table is slightly stale vs globals.css, which is authoritative). (2) The native Swift app (/juno-app) executed a "v2 reset" (docs/DESIGN.md): dark-first graphite surfaces (dark canvas hsl(30 6% 6%)), SF Pro as working type with serif reserved for the single greeting moment, terracotta #C6613F as the one accent, translucent hairline borders instead of boxed sections, tighter radii (7/10/12/14/16/22), 8-pt spacing grid, and Liquid Glass only on floating chrome. Its tokens live in Juno/DesignSystem/Theme.swift (Juno enum), components in Components.swift, and the dot-signature brand layer in Signature.swift. The v2 graphite direction is explicitly modeled on Linear/Raycast/ChatGPT-desktop and is the stated reference for native clients — on Windows, SF Pro's role maps to Segoe UI Variable. Shared invariants across both: the terracotta/coral accent (swappable: coral/teal/violet/amber/sage), the 5px-dot/3px-gap dot signature (ThinkingDots, DotMatrixMark, DotIdenticon, DotFillBar, AsciiHero, VoiceOrb), the flat-transcript law (no gloss/glass inside message content), the same three easing curves, press-scale 0.97 feedback, and mono metadata voice ("N tokens · $X").

summary: The V1 sync protocol is a brand-new (migration 20260716200000) cursor-based change-log system layered on top of the existing REST API. Core design: Postgres triggers (juno_record_account_change) on 20 tables write append-only AccountChange rows (BIGSERIAL cursor, per-account) and maintain an EntityRevision table (per-entity monotonic revision + deletedAt tombstone). Clients: (1) GET /api/v1/bootstrap for account snapshot + currentChangeCursor, (2) page GET /api/v1/changes?after=cursor for typed change envelopes (no entity bodies), (3) fetch full bodies from the legacy /api/* REST endpoints (which accept the same native Bearer token via getCurrentUser), (4) write through POST /api/v1/mutations — idempotent (clientMutationId receipt table), optimistic-concurrency (baseRevision vs EntityRevision, 409 revision_conflict), run in a Serializable transaction. The 'SSE' at GET /api/v1/changes/stream is NOT a long-lived push channel: it emits ready → optional cursor → done and closes immediately (no heartbeats exist in the code) — it is a poll-shaped wakeup check. Compaction is not implemented: compactionFloorCursor is hardcoded "0" and no 410 path exists anywhere in the server source; the field is reserved for future truncation (client should treat cursor < floor as 'full resync via bootstrap'). Auth: 10-min HS256 JWT access token (aud "juno-native") + 30-day rotating refresh token with reuse-detection that revokes the whole device session. The existing Swift Mac app does NOT use this protocol — it does full-list polling of legacy REST endpoints with upsert-only merge; the v1 protocol appears purpose-built for new clients like the Windows app.
contracts: AUTH HEADER (all /api/v1/* except models, and all legacy /api/* when used natively): `Authorization: Bearer <JWT>`. JWT: HS256, claims { sub: userId, sid: deviceSessionId, sv: sessionVersion, typ: "native_access", iss: <app origin>, aud: "juno-native", iat, exp }; TTL 600s (NATIVE_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS in src/lib/native-auth-core.ts). Missing header → 401 unauthenticated; expired → 401 token_expired; revoked device → 401 device_revoked.

RESPONSE ENVELOPE (src/lib/api-v1.ts): every v1 response carries headers `X-Juno-Request-Id: req_<uuid>` and `X-Juno-Contract-Version: 1.0.0` (CONTRACT_VERSION). Error body: { "error": { "code", "message", "requestId", "retryable": bool, "retryAfterMs": null, "details"?: object } }. Codes seen in code: server_unavailable (500, retryable:true — the ONLY retryable:true code), invalid_request (400), unauthenticated/token_expired/device_revoked/token_reuse_detected (401), invalid_grant (400), not_found (404), revision_conflict (409), idempotency_key_reused (409).

GET /api/v1/bootstrap → 200 { profile: { id, name, email, image }, subscription: { plan: lowercase Plan ("free"|"pro"|"max"|"max20"|"owner"), status: lowercase SubStatus ("active"…), currentPeriodEnd: ISO|null, cancelAtPeriodEnd: bool } (defaults {plan:"free",status:"active",currentPeriodEnd:null,cancelAtPeriodEnd:false}), usage: { period: "YYYY-MM", messageCount: int, promptTokens: string, completionTokens: string } (BigInts serialized via .toString()), settings: <raw Prisma Settings row or null> { id, userId, theme: "LIGHT"|"DARK"|"SYSTEM", accent, defaultModel, customInstructions, responseLanguage, uiLocale, personality, memoryEnabled, voiceId: string|null, favoriteModels: string[], emailBudgetAlerts, emailWeeklyDigest, createdAt, updatedAt }, featureFlags: {}, currentChangeCursor: string (decimal bigint), compactionFloorCursor: "0", modelManifestVersion: "v1-<16 hex chars>", contractVersion: "1.0.0", minimumClientVersions: { macOS: "3.0.0" }, announcements: [] }.

GET /api/v1/changes?after=<cursor>&limit=<n> → 200 { after: string, changes: ChangeEnvelope[], nextCursor: string, compactionFloorCursor: "0", hasMore: bool }. `after` default 0; must match /^(0|[1-9][0-9]{0,30})$/ (no sign, no leading zeros, no whitespace). `limit` default 100, min 1, max 500 (MAX_CHANGE_PAGE_SIZE). Invalid cursor/limit → 400 invalid_request. ChangeEnvelope (src/lib/sync-protocol.ts changeEnvelope): { cursor: string, entityType: string, entityId: string, parentEntityId: string|null, revision: number, operation: "upsert"|"delete", changedAt: ISO-8601 }. nextCursor = last row's cursor (or `after` when page empty); poll again with after=nextCursor while hasMore.

entityType values (from trigger installation in prisma/migrations/20260716200000_account_change_log/migration.sql): "profile" (User UPDATE), "settings", "subscription", "folder", "conversation", "message" (parentEntityId=conversationId), "message_version" (parent=messageId), "attachment", "artifact" (parent=conversationId), "artifact_version" (parent=artifactId), "project", "memory" (MemoryEntry), "saved_prompt", "connection", "usage", "share", "announcement_dismissal", "scheduled_task", "code_device", "code_task", "code_task_event" (parent=taskId).

REVISION RULES (trigger juno_record_account_change): EntityRevision keyed (accountId, entityType, entityId); first write inserts revision=1, every subsequent INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE increments revision by 1; DELETE also stamps deletedAt (tombstone kept forever). Every write from ANY surface (website, chat streaming, v1 mutations, admin) fires the trigger, so the change feed is complete. Cursors are a single global BIGSERIAL — per-account sequences are strictly increasing but NOT contiguous (tests/sync-protocol.test.ts: "does not assume contiguity").

COMPACTION/410: compactionFloorCursor is hardcoded "0" in both bootstrap and changes routes; there is NO 410 response and no compaction job anywhere in the server source today. The field is a forward-compat reservation — client should implement: if my saved cursor < compactionFloorCursor, discard local change state and re-bootstrap. Do not build against a 410 status; it does not exist yet.

GET /api/v1/changes/stream?after=<cursor> (SSE, src/app/api/v1/changes/stream/route.ts) → headers { Content-Type: "text/event-stream; charset=utf-8", Cache-Control: "no-store", X-Accel-Buffering: "no", X-Juno-Contract-Version: "1.0.0" }. Emits exactly, in order: `event: ready` + data `{"after":"<echoed cursor>"}`; then IFF latest account cursor > after: `event: cursor` + data `{"cursor":"<latest>"}`; then `event: done` + data `{}`; then the stream CLOSES. There are NO heartbeat/ping events and no long-lived connection — it is a snapshot wakeup poll. On `cursor`, client should GET /api/v1/changes.

POST /api/v1/mutations — request (zod .strict()): { clientMutationId: uuid, baseRevision: int ≥ 0, operation: <discriminated union on "type"> }. Operation types and payloads (all .strict()): conversation.create { clientEntityId?: uuid, title?: string 1..200 }; conversation.rename { entityId: string 1..200, title: string 1..200 }; conversation.update { entityId, patch: { title?: 1..200, pinned?: bool, projectId?: string|null, folderId?: string|null } }; conversation.delete { entityId }; project.create { clientEntityId?, name: 1..160, instructions: max 50000 default "" }; project.update { entityId, name?, instructions? }; project.delete { entityId }; memory.create { clientEntityId?, content: 1..20000 }; memory.update { entityId, content: 1..20000 }; memory.delete { entityId }; settings.update { patch: { theme?: "LIGHT"|"DARK"|"SYSTEM", accent?: 1..40, defaultModel?: 1..200, customInstructions?: max 50000, responseLanguage?: 1..80, uiLocale?: 1..40, personality?: 1..80, memoryEnabled?: bool, voiceId?: string(max 200)|null, favoriteModels?: string[] max 100 } }. NOTE: there are NO folder.* mutation ops — folders are written via legacy REST.

POST /api/v1/mutations — results (200): create ops → { entityMappings: { "<clientEntityId>": "<server cuid>" } (empty {} if clientEntityId omitted), entity: { id, revision } }; rename/update ops → { entity: { id, revision } }; delete ops → { entity: { id, revision, deleted: true } }. `revision` is read back from EntityRevision AFTER the write (trigger already incremented), so a create returns revision 1 and an update from baseRevision N returns N+1.

POST /api/v1/mutations — conflict semantics: create ops require baseRevision === 0 else 400 invalid_request "Create mutations must start at revision zero.". Update/delete: if EntityRevision.deletedAt set → 409 revision_conflict with details { currentRevision, deleted: true }; if revision !== baseRevision → 409 revision_conflict with details { currentRevision }. settings.update on a user with no Settings row requires baseRevision 0 (else 409 revision_conflict details { currentRevision: 0 }); otherwise revision checked against the Settings row's id. conversation.update with projectId/folderId verifies ownership → 404 not_found ("The project/folder was not found."). Idempotency: receipt keyed (accountId, authenticatedDeviceId, clientMutationId) with requestHash = sha256(JSON.stringify(entire request body)); same key + same hash → replays stored result verbatim; same key + different hash → 409 idempotency_key_reused. P2002 unique-violation race resolves post-hoc to replay / idempotency_key_reused / generic revision_conflict ("The mutation raced with another device. Refresh and retry."). Transaction isolation: Serializable.

GET /api/v1/models — auth via getCurrentUser (Bearer OR web session cookie); 401 body { error: { code: "unauthenticated", message: "Authentication is required.", retryable: false, retryAfterMs: null } }. 200 → { manifestVersion: "v1-<digest[0..16]>", contractDigest: <sha256 hex of models JSON>, models: ModelEntry[], generatedAt: ISO } with headers ETag: '"<contractDigest>"' and Cache-Control: "private, max-age=300". ModelEntry (src/lib/model-catalog-api.ts nativeModelCatalog): { id, provider: { id, displayName }, displayName, description: string|null, lifecycle: "deprecated"|"legacy"|"active", availability: "coming_soon"|"available", minimumPlan: lowercase plan, modalities: { input: ["text"] or ["text","image"], output: ["text"|"image"|"video"] }, contextWindowTokens: number, pricing: { class: "premium"|"standard"|"economy", inputPerMillion, outputPerMillion, currency: "USD", source }, supportedReasoningEfforts: string[], reasoning: { supported, canDisable, onOffOnly, supportsProMode }, capabilities: { tools, webSearch, attachments, streaming }, deprecationNote: string|null }.

AUTH ENDPOINTS: POST /api/v1/auth/token { code, codeVerifier (43..256 base64url), redirectUri, installationId (16..200), deviceName, platform, appVersion } .strict() → { tokenType: "Bearer", accessToken, accessTokenExpiresAt: ISO, refreshToken, refreshTokenExpiresAt: ISO, deviceSession: { id, name, createdAt } }. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh { refreshToken: string 32..512 } .strict() → { tokenType: "Bearer", accessToken, accessTokenExpiresAt, refreshToken (ROTATED — new value every call), refreshTokenExpiresAt }. Refresh reuse → 401 token_reuse_detected and the whole device session + token family is revoked. Redirect URI constant: "com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback" (legacy "juno://auth/callback"); PKCE S256 only. Auth-code TTL 2 min; refresh TTL 30 days.

ENTITY-BODY FETCH PAIRING (change feed carries NO bodies; legacy /api/* accept the native Bearer because getCurrentUser in src/lib/session.ts tries authenticateNativeBearer first): entityType "conversation" → GET /api/conversations (list, params q & folderId, → { conversations: ClientConversation[] }) or GET /api/conversations/{entityId} (thread → { conversation, messages: ClientMessage[], artifacts: ClientArtifact[] }); "message"/"artifact" → GET /api/conversations/{parentEntityId} (parent is the conversationId); "message_version" → GET /api/messages/{parentEntityId}/versions; "artifact_version" → refetch thread of the artifact's conversation; "folder" → GET /api/folders → { folders: [{ id, name, createdAt }] }; "project" → GET /api/projects → { projects: [{ id, name, instructions, updatedAt, conversationCount, fileCount, coverUrl }] } or GET /api/projects/{entityId} → { project: { id, name, instructions, updatedAt }, conversations: [{ id, title, pinned, kind?, lastMessageAt }], files: ClientAttachment[] }; "memory" → GET /api/memory → { memories: [{ id, content, source, kind, sourceRef, createdAt }], summary: { content, updatedAt, entryCount }|null }; "settings"/"profile"/"subscription"/"usage" → re-GET /api/v1/bootstrap (there is no GET /api/settings — that route only has PATCH); "saved_prompt" → GET /api/prompts; "connection" → GET /api/connectors.

ClientConversation (src/lib/serializers.ts serializeConversation): { id, title, titleSource, model, kind: "chat"|"code", codeWorkspaceName: string|null, codeWorkspacePath: string|null, pinned: bool, folderId: string|null, projectId: string|null, activeConnectors: string[], archivedAt: ISO|null, lastMessageAt: ISO, createdAt: ISO }. ClientMessage (serializeMessage): { id, role: "user"|"assistant"|"system", content (decrypted), reasoning?, reasoningParts?: string[], model, feedback, createdAt: ISO, attachments: [{ id, kind, fileName, mimeType, size, url (signed view URL), width, height }], sources?, activity?, promptTokens, completionTokens, costUsd? (upper-bound estimate), conversationId, versions?: [{ id, model, createdAt }] }. ClientArtifact (serializeArtifact): { id, identifier, type, title, language, currentVersion, content (latest), versions: [{ version, content, createdAt }], messageId, createdAt, updatedAt }.
behaviors: Sync loop the Windows client should implement: bootstrap → persist currentChangeCursor → page /api/v1/changes (after=cursor, limit up to 500, loop while hasMore) → for each change, group by entityType and refetch bodies from the paired REST endpoint (dedupe: one thread fetch covers many message/artifact changes with the same parentEntityId) → persist nextCursor. Use /api/v1/changes/stream purely as a cheap 'anything new?' probe: it answers instantly and closes; reconnect on your own interval (there is no server push or heartbeat to keep alive).

operation "delete" changes are authoritative tombstones — apply local deletes. Note cascades fan out: deleting a conversation emits a delete change for the conversation AND for every child message/artifact (the trigger resolves accountId for orphaned messages via the EntityRevision fallback).

Mutations: generate clientMutationId (UUID) once per logical user action and reuse it verbatim on every retry — replay is byte-safe only if the retried body is IDENTICAL (hash covers the whole body including baseRevision). Retry on 500 server_unavailable (retryable:true, Serializable-conflict fallout); on 409 revision_conflict read details.currentRevision (and details.deleted) then refetch the entity, rebase, and retry with a NEW clientMutationId.

Create flow: send clientEntityId (client-minted UUID) with *.create, keep the optimistic local row under that UUID, then adopt the server cuid from result.entityMappings[clientEntityId]. baseRevision must be 0 for creates. Track per-entity revision locally: seed from change envelopes (envelope.revision) and mutation results (entity.revision); send it as baseRevision on the next mutation of that entity.

Access token lifecycle: 10-minute JWT; refresh proactively via /api/v1/auth/refresh and atomically persist the rotated refreshToken before using it — serialize refresh calls (a concurrent second use of the old token trips reuse detection and revokes the whole device session, forcing re-login).
Idempotency receipts are scoped to the device session (accountId + authenticatedDeviceId + clientMutationId) — a re-login/new device session cannot replay old receipts.

The Windows client can instead be revision-correct via v1, but the upsert-merge + id-adoption patterns are worth copying for offline resilience.

Conversation title ownership: v1 conversation.rename/update sets titleSource "user" (legacy PATCH sets "manual") which stops server-side auto-titling from overwriting the user's title; conversation.create without title leaves title "New chat" / titleSource "default" so auto-titling can fill it.

GET /api/v1/models supports ETag revalidation (If-None-Match against contractDigest) and 5-minute private caching; bootstrap's modelManifestVersion tells you when to refetch the catalog.

risks: The SSE stream is a stub, not a push channel: it closes right after ready/cursor/done and has no heartbeats despite being under /changes/stream. If the Windows client expects a persistent EventSource it will busy-reconnect; build it as a polled probe (or plain-poll /api/v1/changes) and design for the server later upgrading to a real long-lived stream with the same event names.

compactionFloorCursor is hardcoded "0" and no 410/compaction exists yet — but MutationReceipt/AccountChange tables grow unboundedly, so compaction is clearly coming. Implement floor handling (cursor < floor → full resync) now even though it can never trigger today.

V1 mutations cover only conversation/project/memory/settings — folders, saved prompts, archiving (archivedAt), activeConnectors, and message writes still go through legacy REST (which does NOT bump anything client-visible beyond the trigger-driven change feed). A hybrid client must accept that its own legacy-REST writes come back to it as change-feed events with revisions it didn't originate.

Theme enum casing diverges: v1 settings.update takes "LIGHT"|"DARK"|"SYSTEM" (Prisma enum), legacy PATCH /api/settings takes "light"|"dark"|"system", and legacy has extra fields (emailBudgetAlerts, emailWeeklyDigest) v1 lacks, plus stricter customInstructions limit (4000 vs 50000 in v1). Pick one write path per field to avoid ping-ponging.

Serializable-transaction aborts (Prisma P2034) are NOT caught in the mutations route — they surface as 500 server_unavailable retryable:true. Safe to retry with the same clientMutationId, but expect these under multi-device write contention.

Refresh-token rotation with reuse detection revokes the ENTIRE device session on a double-spend — a Tauri app that persists the refresh token non-atomically (crash between receiving and writing) or fires concurrent refreshes will log the user out. Mutex + write-before-use is mandatory.

Ownership guard (src/lib/db.ts): dev servers throw on user-scoped queries missing a userId filter; if you add server endpoints for the Windows client, scope every query or use prismaUnguarded deliberately.
requestHash = sha256(JSON.stringify(body)) is key-order-sensitive — a retry must resend the byte-identical JSON (same serializer, same field order) or it will 409 idempotency_key_reused.

The migration is dated 2026-07-16 (yesterday) — this protocol is unshipped/fresh; verify the production DB actually has the triggers before relying on the change feed, and expect contract churn (CONTRACT_VERSION is still 1.0.0 everywhere).

summary: Juno CODE MODE remote protocol is a dumb-relay task queue over plain JSON/HTTP (no server push): a Mac "host" device registers and heartbeats via POST /api/code/devices, long-polls GET /api/code/queue for queued tasks, claims them (queued->running), executes them with the local CodeAgentEngine, and streams progress by POSTing append-only event batches to /api/code/tasks/[id]/events (server assigns seq numbers transactionally and returns pending control events — approval_response/cancel_request — past afterControlSeq). Monitoring clients render a task by polling GET /api/code/tasks/[id]?afterSeq=N and answer approvals via POST .../respond. Model inference never uses provider keys on the client: the app builds provider-native requests (Anthropic Messages / OpenAI chat.completions / OpenAI Responses) and POSTs them to /api/agent/<provider>/<path>, an authenticated transparent proxy that injects the server-side key and pipes the provider's native SSE stream back untouched; usage bills the account plan via POST /api/agent/usage (reserve/record/refund, fail-open except HTTP 402). Auth everywhere is the next-auth v5 session cookie (authjs.session-token), obtained via CSRF+credentials POST or a juno://auth?token= web handoff. Key server files: juno/src/app/api/code/**, juno/src/lib/code-remote.ts, juno/src/app/api/agent/[...path]/route.ts, juno/src/app/api/agent/usage/route.ts, docs/code-remote.md (accurate). Client references: juno-app/Juno/Services/Code/RemoteCodeAPI.swift, RemoteCodeModels.swift, RemoteCodeHost.swift, CodeAgentEngine.swift, Juno/Services/Providers/ProviderClient.swift, Juno/Services/Backend/AgentUsageReporter.swift, and the portable TS core in juno-app/core/src/ (server.ts WebSocket sidecar, providers/proxy.ts, usage.ts, types.ts).
contracts: AUTH (all endpoints): next-auth v5 (Auth.js) session cookie. Native sign-in flow (juno-app/Juno/Services/Backend/AuthSession.swift): (1) GET /api/auth/csrf -> {csrfToken} + CSRF cookie; (2) form-encoded POST /api/auth/callback/credentials with fields csrfToken, email, password, json=true -> sets `authjs.session-token` cookie (name is `__Secure-authjs.session-token` on HTTPS); (3) persist the cookie VALUE, reinstall on launch, validate via GET /api/auth/session. Alternative: open <base>/app-auth in a system browser; the page deep-links back `juno://auth?token=<sessionTokenValue>` which is adopted as the cookie (WebAuthService.swift). Unauthenticated -> 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}. Resources of other users -> 404 {"error":"Not found"}.

POST /api/code/devices — register/heartbeat. Body (zod): {deviceId?: string, name: string(trim,1-200), platform: "macos" (z.literal — ONLY "macos" accepted), workspaces: [{name: string(trim,1-200), path: string(trim,1-1000)}] max 100}. Upserts by id when deviceId is owned by the user, else by unique (userId,name); always refreshes lastSeenAt+workspaces. 200 {device: {id, name, platform, workspaces, lastSeenAt: ISO8601}} (no `online` field on POST). Invalid body -> 400 {"error":"Invalid input"}.

GET /api/code/devices — 200 {devices: [{id, name, platform, workspaces: [{name,path}], lastSeenAt: ISO, online: boolean}]}, ordered lastSeenAt desc; online = now - lastSeenAt <= 120000 ms (ONLINE_WINDOW_MS in src/lib/code-remote.ts).

POST /api/code/tasks — queue a task. Body: {deviceId: string(1-200), workspacePath: string(trim,1-1000), workspaceName?: string(trim,max 200), title?: string(trim,1-200), prompt: string(trim,1-100000)}. deviceId must be a device owned by the user else 404. Defaults: title = prompt.slice(0,60), workspaceName = "". 201 {task: <Task>}.

Task JSON shape (serializeTask): {id, deviceId, workspacePath, workspaceName, title, prompt, status, lastSeq: number, createdAt: ISO, updatedAt: ISO}. status enum TASK_STATUSES = ["queued","running","awaiting_approval","done","failed","cancelled"]. Lifecycle: queued -> running (claim) -> awaiting_approval <-> running -> done | failed | cancelled.
GET /api/code/tasks?deviceId=&status=&limit=30 — 200 {tasks: [<Task>]} createdAt desc; limit clamped 1..100 (default 30); invalid status string -> 400.

GET /api/code/tasks/[id]?afterSeq=N — 200 {task: <Task>, events: [{seq: number, kind: string, payload: object, createdAt: ISO}]} with seq > afterSeq, ascending, capped at 500 rows. This polling endpoint IS the "event stream" — there is no SSE/WebSocket for tasks.

POST /api/code/tasks/[id]/claim — body {deviceId: string(1-200)}. Atomic updateMany where {status:"queued", deviceId, userId} -> running; also appends event {kind:"status",payload:{status:"running"}}. Success 200 {task}. Task exists but not queued -> 409 {"error":"not_queued"}. Unknown task -> 404.

POST /api/code/tasks/[id]/events — host append. Body: {events: [{kind: <EVENT_KINDS enum>, payload: object}] max 500, status?: <TASK_STATUSES>, afterControlSeq?: int >= 0}. Raw request body over 262144 bytes (256KB) -> 413 {"error":"Payload too large"}. Server transactionally increments task.lastSeq by events.length and assigns contiguous seq numbers. 200 {lastSeq: number, control: [{seq, kind, payload}]} — control contains events of kind approval_response|cancel_request with seq > afterControlSeq ascending (empty array when afterControlSeq omitted... precisely: [] when afterControlSeq is undefined; route defaults it to 0 so control is always returned to this endpoint).

EVENT_KINDS (src/lib/code-remote.ts): "status","user","text","tool","file_change","approval_request","approval_response","cancel_request","error","done". Payloads: status {status}; user {text} (initial prompt echo, host posts first); text {text} (assistant prose, host batches ~1/s, clients concatenate consecutive text events); tool {name, summary, detail?} (human-readable, e.g. "Read src/foo.ts", "$ npm test — exit 0"); file_change {path, changeKind: "edit"|"create"|"delete", added: int, removed: int, diff?: string (unified diff, cap 40KB)}; approval_request {requestId, summary, risk: "neutral"|"destructive"|"outside", detail?}; approval_response {requestId, approve: boolean}; cancel_request {}; error {message}; done {finishReason: string, promptTokens?: int, completionTokens?: int}. finishReason values (Swift FinishReason rawValues, Juno/Models/CoreTypes.swift): stop, length, tool_calls, sensitive, model_context_window_exceeded, network_error, user_stopped, error, unknown.

POST /api/code/tasks/[id]/respond — body {requestId: string(1-200), approve: boolean}. Appends approval_response event; iff task.status === "awaiting_approval" also appends {kind:"status",payload:{status:"running"}} and flips task to running via guarded conditional transition (updateMany where status=awaiting_approval — a concurrently finished task cannot be revived). 200 {lastSeq}.

POST /api/code/tasks/[id]/cancel — no body. Appends cancel_request {}; if the task is still "queued" also appends status cancelled and sets task.status=cancelled immediately; a running task is left for the host to finalize (host receives cancel_request in `control`). 200 {task}.

GET /api/code/queue?deviceId=<id> — long-poll for the oldest (createdAt asc) queued task on that device. Server rechecks DB every 1500ms for up to 25000ms (route maxDuration=30). 200 {task: <Task>} or {task: null}. Missing deviceId -> 400; device not owned -> 404. Swift client uses a 40s request timeout.

GET /api/code/workspaces — 200 {workspaces: [{id, name, path, lastOpenedAt: ISO}]} ordered lastOpenedAt desc. PUT /api/code/workspaces — body {workspaces: [{id: string(1-64), name: string(trim,1-300), path: string(trim,1-1000), lastOpenedAt?: ISO datetime}] max 200} — MIRROR-SYNC: deletes the user's server rows whose path is not in the payload, then upserts by (userId,path). 200 {ok:true, count}. This table (CodeWorkspace) is separate from CodeDevice.workspaces and is what the website sidebar reads (fetch("/api/code/workspaces") in juno/src/components/app/app-sidebar.tsx).

POST /api/agent/<provider>/<path> — transparent authenticated model proxy (juno/src/app/api/agent/[...path]/route.ts, runtime nodejs, maxDuration 300). provider must be a key of PROVIDERS (src/lib/providers.ts): anthropic, openai, zhipu, moonshot, google, meta, deepseek, mistral, xai, seedance, minimax, mimo, qwen, longcat — else 400 {"error":"Unknown provider."}. Allowed forward paths: kind anthropic -> only "v1/messages"; kind openai -> "chat/completions" for all, plus "responses" for provider==="openai" only; else 403 {"error":"Endpoint not allowed."}. Request body = provider-native JSON forwarded VERBATIM (set stream:true yourself for SSE). Server-injected headers: anthropic -> x-api-key: <server key>, anthropic-version: <client's header or "2023-06-01">, anthropic-beta forwarded if the client sent one; openai-kind -> authorization: Bearer <server key>; content-type: application/json. Response: upstream body piped back untouched (native Anthropic Messages SSE / OpenAI chat chunks), upstream status preserved, content-type copied, cache-control: no-store. Gating for non-OWNER plans: rate limit key agent:<userId> 120 req / 60s -> 429 {"error":"Rate limit exceeded. Try again shortly."}; budget check -> 402 {error: <human copy>, code: "QUOTA_EXCEEDED"}. Provider key missing on server -> 502 {"error":"<label> isn't configured on the server."}; upstream fetch failure -> 502 {"error":"Upstream provider request failed."}. Clients pass a dummy api key ("proxy") to their own request builders; the proxy discards inbound auth headers.

POST /api/agent/usage — plan accounting for Code turns (same session cookie). {phase:"start"} -> checks € budget then consumeMessage; blocked -> 402 {error, code:"QUOTA_EXCEEDED", quota?}; ok -> 200 {ok:true, quota}. {phase:"record", promptTokens: int, completionTokens: int, model: string} -> adds tokens to the period aggregate + writes ApiSpend ledger row (kind "code", source "app") -> {ok:true}. {phase:"refund"} -> returns a reserved message when the turn produced no billable output -> {ok:true, quota}. Bad JSON -> 400 {"error":"Invalid JSON body."}; unknown phase -> 400 {"error":"Unknown phase."}. This route is more specific than /api/agent/[...path] so it wins over the catch-all.

GET /api/models — 200 {models: [...], manifestVersion} (session-cookie auth; sorted [lab asc, intelligence desc, released desc, name asc]; the Mac app trusts the order verbatim). This is where a client learns which providers/models are server-configured before offering proxy models.

Local sidecar protocol (juno-app/core/src/server.ts, NDJSON over WebSocket on 127.0.0.1:<port>, optional shared secret as ?token= query param, timing-safe compare, bad token -> close code 4001 'invalid token'). client->server message types: configure_backend {baseUrl (e.g. https://chat.liams.dev/api/agent, no trailing slash), cookie (full Cookie header value), models: BackendCatalogModel[]}; start {cwd, model?, mode?, provider?}; resume {sessionId, mode?}; prompt {text}; approval {callId, decision: 'allow'|'allow_always'|'deny'}; set_mode {mode: 'plan'|'ask'|'auto-edit'|'full'}; undo; diff {sinceTurn?}; list_models; list_sessions; delete_session {sessionId}; rename_session {sessionId, title}; abort. server->client: {type:'event', event: AgentEvent}; {type:'models', providers: ProviderListing[]}; {type:'diff', patch}; {type:'undo_result', restored}; {type:'sessions', sessions: SessionMeta[]}; {type:'protocol_error', message}. Backend-proxied provider ids are prefixed "backend/<provider>". BackendCatalogModel = {provider, providerName?, kind: 'anthropic'|'openai', model, label, available, reason?, vision?, contextWindow?}.

AgentEvent union (juno-app/core/src/types.ts): session_started {sessionId,cwd,provider,model,mode}; turn_started {turnIndex}; assistant_delta {text}; assistant_message {text}; tool_started {callId,name,input,risk: 'safe'|'edit'|'command'|'sensitive'}; tool_finished {callId,name,output,isError,durationMs}; tool_denied {callId,name,reason}; approval_requested {request: {callId,toolName,input,risk,summary}}; approval_resolved {callId,decision}; files_changed {turnIndex,paths}; mode_changed {mode}; turn_finished {turnIndex,stopReason,usage:{inputTokens,outputTokens}}; error {message}.
behaviors: Host lifecycle (RemoteCodeHost.swift): register on start then heartbeat every 60s; persist a locally generated UUID device id, then overwrite it with the server's canonical device.id after the first successful upsert; heartbeat errors back off 5s doubling to max 120s; skip cycles while signed out.

Queue loop: long-poll GET /api/code/queue (client timeout 40s > server 25s window); on a task, POST claim FIRST — a 409 not_queued means another host won the race and is silently skipped; exactly one task runs at a time (polling pauses while a run is awaited inline); poll errors back off 3s doubling to max 60s.

Event forwarding: the host buffers outgoing events; assistant text deltas accumulate in a string buffer that is folded into ONE "text" event whenever a tool/file_change/approval event is appended or at flush time (order preserved; clients concatenate consecutive text events). A flush loop POSTs the pending batch every ~1.2s — including EMPTY batches, so cancel_request control events reach the host promptly mid-run. Exactly one events-POST in flight at a time (serialized with a busy flag); on failure the batch is re-inserted at the front of the queue so no events are lost. afterControlSeq is advanced to the max control seq seen and sent on every POST.

Run start: host posts {kind:"user",payload:{text: task.prompt}} as the first event. Pre-run failures (empty prompt, workspace path not registered on this Mac, sandbox access lost, no data store) post error {message} + status failed with 3 retries.

Approval round-trip: host generates requestId = UUID; posts approval_request {requestId, summary: action.shortLabel, risk ("outside" for outside-workspace, "destructive" for delete/destructive-classified commands, else "neutral"), detail: prompt} PLUS a status awaiting_approval event, with the POST's status field also set to awaiting_approval (3 retries). Then it polls every 2s (empty event batches) looking for a matching approval_response in `control`. Timeout after 300s -> treated as deny, and the host posts a tool event ("Approval timed out after 5 minutes — denied") + status running to resume itself (no /respond happened so the server never flipped the status back). An explicit client deny appends a tool event "Denied from the remote client: <label>".

Cancellation: a cancel_request in control sets a flag and calls engine.stop(); the finalize step then reports status cancelled with done {finishReason:"user_stopped"}. A queued (unclaimed) task is cancelled server-side immediately by POST /cancel.

Finalize: drain text buffer, append done {finishReason, promptTokens?, completionTokens?} then a status event; POST with the final status field and 4 retries. Outcome mapping: engine error -> failed (+error event); FinishReason.userStopped -> cancelled; otherwise done.

Monitoring client rendering: poll GET /tasks/[id]?afterSeq=<last seen seq>; tolerate unknown event kinds (Swift keeps kind as a raw string and only types known ones); concatenate consecutive text events into one bubble; approval_request renders an approve/deny sheet that POSTs /respond {requestId, approve}.

Agent loop billing (CodeAgentEngine.swift, mirrors core/src/agent.ts): reserve ONE plan message per user turn (not per loop iteration) via POST /api/agent/usage {phase:"start"} BEFORE any model call; only a genuine HTTP 402 blocks the turn — 401/5xx/network failures FAIL OPEN. At turn end, if cumulative usage has input>0 or output>0 POST {phase:"record", model, promptTokens, completionTokens}, else POST {phase:"refund"} (fire-and-forget). Billing gate: account mode only — signed in, backend not mock, BYOK toggle off, AND no local keychain key for the model's provider (a stored key means ProviderRouter streams direct and the plan must not be charged).

Provider routing (ProviderClient.swift): if a local (BYOK) API key exists for the provider -> stream straight to the vendor; otherwise stream through <base>/api/agent/<provider> with placeholder apiKey "proxy" and the session cookie riding the shared cookie store. Path appended by adapter: anthropic -> v1/messages (header anthropic-version: 2023-06-01, anthropic-beta: computer-use-2025-01-24 when computer use is on); openai-compat -> chat/completions; OpenAI gpt-*-pro models -> responses (Responses API only). Transient failures = 429/5xx/NSURLErrorDomain (retryable); 401/402/403 are hard failures.

Agent loop mechanics worth replicating: max 30 iterations per user turn; provider usage snapshots are CUMULATIVE within one HTTP request so they are max-merged per request, but each loop iteration is a fresh request so the turn total SUMS request totals; loop continues only while finish == tool_calls with collected calls; per-iteration message shows that request's tokens while the summed total is what gets recorded.

Sidecar behaviors: one turn at a time per connection ({type:'prompt'} while running -> protocol_error "a turn is already running"); on WS close all pending approvals resolve to 'deny' and the session aborts so the loop can persist; configure_backend immediately re-sends the merged {type:'models'} catalog (backend-proxied providers listed first and authoritative — BYOK duplicates hidden when a backend catalog exists); resume reattaches with the provider stored in the session's meta.json.

Website behavior: the web app sidebar loads GET /api/code/workspaces to show the same Projects list as the Mac app; the Mac app pushes that list with PUT /api/code/workspaces (BackendClient.putCodeWorkspaces) as a full replace, and tags Code conversations with codeWorkspaceName/codeWorkspacePath on POST /api/conversations.
reuse: /Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/core/src/ — the entire TypeScript agent core is directly reusable in a Tauri sidecar (Node) process: server.ts (WebSocket NDJSON protocol + connection handling), agent.ts (loop with reserve/record/refund sequencing), session.ts (SessionStore: ~/.juno/sessions/<id>/{meta.json,messages.json,events.jsonl}, JUNO_HOME env override), types.ts (AgentEvent/ChatMessage/PermissionMode/ApprovalRequest — copy verbatim as the Windows app's shared types), providers/proxy.ts (BackendConfig, BACKEND_PROVIDER_PREFIX='backend/', proxyProviderListings, createProxyProvider — exactly how to route through /api/agent with only a cookie), usage.ts (BackendUsageReporter with correct fail-open semantics). Strip: nothing structural; it depends on node:fs/os/path/crypto + 'ws', so it must run in the Tauri sidecar or be re-hosted on Rust, not in the WebView.

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app/Juno/Services/Code/RemoteCodeModels.swift — transliterate to TypeScript as-is: RemoteTaskStatus, RemoteEventKind, typed payload accessors, and the RemoteOutgoingEvent factory helpers (status/user/text/tool/fileChange/approvalRequest/error/done) define every wire field name. Keep the 'unknown kind stays a raw string' decoding rule.
