risks: POST /api/code/devices hard-codes platform as z.literal("macos") — a Windows machine CANNOT register as a host device today; the backend schema must be widened (e.g. z.enum(["macos","windows"])) before the Tauri app can execute remote tasks. As a pure task-queueing/monitoring client the Windows app works unchanged (deviceId always targets a Mac host).

PUT /api/code/workspaces is a destructive per-USER mirror-sync (deletes any path not in the payload) and the Mac app already owns that list — if the Windows client also PUTs its own workspace list the two apps will delete each other's projects on every sync. For task targeting use the per-DEVICE workspace list from GET /api/code/devices (CodeDevice.workspaces), which is keyed by device and safe.

There is NO server push for task events — no SSE, no WebSocket. "Streaming" a task is polling GET /tasks/[id]?afterSeq=N (events capped at 500/request) and the host piggybacks control delivery on its ~1.2s events POSTs. Budget polling intervals accordingly; don't design for push.

The /api/agent proxy DOES stream (native provider SSE piped through). The response is whatever the provider sends — parse Anthropic Messages events for /anthropic/v1/messages and OpenAI chunk format for /

/chat/completions. Set stream:true in the request body yourself; the proxy forwards the body verbatim and only whitelists paths v1/messages, chat/completions, and (openai only) responses.

Auth is a session COOKIE, not a bearer token: Tauri's WebView fetch won't automatically share cookies with a Node sidecar — replicate the Swift approach: capture the authjs.session-token value (CSRF+credentials POST, or the /app-auth deep-link handoff juno://auth?token=…, cookie name __Secure-authjs.session-token on HTTPS) and attach it as an explicit Cookie header on every backend request (this is exactly what core/src/providers/proxy.ts and usage.ts expect via BackendConfig.cookie).

Quota/limits handling must copy the fail-open rule: for /api/agent/usage only HTTP 402 (code QUOTA_EXCEEDED) blocks a turn — 401/5xx/network errors must NOT surface as quota errors. /api/agent itself can also return 402 QUOTA_EXCEEDED and 429 (rate limit 120 req/60s per user for non-OWNER plans; multi-iteration agent turns can hit it — treat 429 as transient/retryable).

Events POST bodies over 256KB are rejected with 413; unified diffs in file_change should be capped (~40KB per the protocol doc, enforced host-side) and batches kept small. On 413 the Swift host would requeue and retry forever — chunk large diffs proactively.
Claim races are expected: 409 {error:"not_queued"} is benign (another host/session won) and must be swallowed silently, not surfaced as an error.

Approval timeout is HOST policy, not server: the server never times out awaiting_approval. A host must self-resume (post status running) after its 5-minute deny, or the task hangs in awaiting_approval forever. Conversely /respond appends approval_response even for tasks no longer awaiting — hosts must match responses by requestId, not assume ordering.

The seq counter is authoritative server-side (transactional increment of task.lastSeq); clients must never invent seq values and must tolerate unknown event kinds from newer servers (keep kind as a raw string, like RemoteCodeModels.swift).
Server-side long-poll on /api/code/queue holds ≤25s with maxDuration 30 — client HTTP timeout must exceed 25s (Swift uses 40s) or every poll aborts early.

Environment assumptions: provider keys live in server env (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY etc., see juno/src/lib/providers.ts); Anthropic proxy base is hard-coded https://api.anthropic.com; anthropic-beta is forwarded (the Mac app sends computer-use-2025-01-24 for computer use). Next.js route handlers use runtime nodejs; agent proxy maxDuration 300 caps a single streamed request at 5 minutes.

What stays local (never synced): security-scoped folder access/bookmarks, full session transcripts (SwiftData conversations on Mac; ~/.juno/sessions for the TS core), checkpoints/undo state, staged pending edits, terminal output. The server stores only devices, tasks, the append-only event log, and the {id,name,path,lastOpenedAt} workspace mirror — design the Windows app's local store accordingly.

summary: Juno's account REST surface is a flat set of Next.js App Router JSON routes under /api, all gated by getCurrentUser() (src/lib/session.ts) which accepts EITHER the Auth.js v5 session cookie (authjs.session-token / __Secure-authjs.session-token, JWT strategy, ~30-day expiry) OR an `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` native device-session token (authenticateNativeBearer, src/lib/native-auth.ts) — a presented Bearer never falls back to the cookie. Every route returns `{"error":"Unauthorized"}` 401 when unauthenticated and `{"error":"<msg>"}` on failure; a few add `code` (chat quota) or 429 rate-limit bodies. There is NO pagination anywhere: lists are hard-capped (conversations 200, library 300, prompts 200, artifacts 200) and ordered server-side. Uploads are direct multipart/form-data to the Next server (no presigned-S3 client flow); download URLs come back already resolved by getViewUrl() (local proxy `/api/files/<key>`, S3 public URL, or 1-hour presigned GET). Artifacts are typed text blobs (HTML|REACT|CODE|MARKDOWN|SVG|MERMAID) with full version history inline; only MARKDOWN artifacts export to docx/xlsx/pptx. The Swift app (cookie-authed via HTTPCookieStorage) uses the conversations/projects/folders(read)/memory(read)/library(read)/models/settings(PATCH)/profile(stats,usage,avatar)/connectors subset; prompts, share, import, memory edit-by-NL, artifacts, attachments CRUD, upload, announcements, and account export/delete are web-only today. juno-app/docs/BACKEND_API.md is accurate but stale in spots: it predates Bearer auth, the conversation kind/codeWorkspace/archived fields, titleSource/activeConnectors in ClientConversation, `manifestVersion` on /api/models, and it wrongly says search covers message content (messages are now encrypted at rest; `q` matches titles only).
contracts: AUTH (all routes below): session cookie (authjs.session-token | __Secure-authjs.session-token) OR header `Authorization: Bearer <native access token>`. Unauthed -> 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}. Validation -> 400 {"error":"Invalid input"} (or first zod issue message). Not owned -> 404 {"error":"Not found"}. Rate limited -> 429 {"error":"<msg>"}. Owner emails (isOwnerEmail) bypass all rate limits.

GET /api/conversations?q=<title substring>&folderId=<cuid> -> 200 {conversations: ClientConversation[]} — ordered pinned desc then lastMessageAt desc, take 200, archived EXCLUDED (archivedAt null only); `q` matches title only (insensitive contains). ClientConversation = {id, title, titleSource:'default'|'ai'|'manual'|'imported'(coerced), model, kind:'chat'|'code', codeWorkspaceName:string|null, codeWorkspacePath:string|null, pinned:boolean, folderId:string|null, projectId:string|null, activeConnectors:string[], archivedAt:iso|null, lastMessageAt:iso, createdAt:iso}.
POST /api/conversations body {kind?:'code'(anything else->'chat'), codeWorkspaceName?:string<=300, codeWorkspacePath?:string<=300} -> 201 {conversation: ClientConversation} (titleSource 'default').
DELETE /api/conversations (no body) -> {ok:true} — deletes ALL of the user's conversations.

GET /api/conversations/{id} -> 200 {conversation: ClientConversation, messages: ClientMessage[], artifacts: ClientArtifact[]} — messages asc by createdAt. ClientMessage = {id, role:'USER'|'ASSISTANT'|'SYSTEM', content, reasoning?, reasoningParts?:string[], model:string|null, feedback:'UP'|'DOWN'|null, createdAt:iso, attachments:ClientAttachment[], sources?:[{title,url,snippet}], activity?:[{id,kind:'context'|'model'|'reasoning'|'search'|'visit'|'write'|'usage'|'done'|'warning'|'tool',title,detail?,url?,createdAt}], promptTokens:number|null, completionTokens:number|null, costUsd?:number (reload = upper-bound estimate), conversationId, versions?:[{id,model,createdAt:iso}] (metadata only; content via GET /api/messages/{id}/versions)}. ClientAttachment = {id, kind:'IMAGE'|'FILE', fileName, mimeType, size, url (pre-resolved view URL), width:number|null, height:number|null}.

PATCH /api/conversations/{id} body (all optional) {title:1..200, pinned:bool, archived:bool (maps to archivedAt; true stamps only on null->now transition, false clears), folderId:cuid|null (ownership-checked, 404 'Folder not found'), projectId:cuid|null (ownership-checked, 404 'Project not found'), kind:'chat'|'code', codeWorkspaceName:1..300|null, codeWorkspacePath:1..1000|null} -> {conversation: ClientConversation}. Setting title forces titleSource:'manual'.
DELETE /api/conversations/{id} -> {ok:true}.

POST /api/conversations/{id}/fork body {atMessageId:cuid} -> {conversation: ClientConversation} — new conversation titled '<truncated 110> (branch)', titleSource 'manual', copies messages createdAt<=anchor verbatim (no versions/attachments/artifacts), sets forkedFromId. 404 {"error":"Message not found in this conversation."} for bad anchor.

POST /api/conversations/{id}/title body {phase?:'first_user'|'thinking'|'writing'|'completed'|'stopped', messages?:[{role:'USER'|'ASSISTANT',content:<=50000}] max 8} -> {title, titleSource:'ai'|'default'|'manual', renamed:bool, projectId?:cuid|null, projectName:null}. Rate limit 20/60s -> 429 {"error":"Too many title updates."}. Never overrides manual titles; may async-rename the owning project.

GET /api/folders -> {folders:[{id,name,createdAt}]} (asc createdAt; createdAt is JSON-serialized Date, iso). POST /api/folders body {name:1..60} -> 201 {folder:{id,name,createdAt}}. PATCH /api/folders/{id} body {name:1..60} -> {folder:{id,name}}. DELETE /api/folders/{id} -> {ok:true} (conversations keep existing, folderId set null via FK).

GET /api/projects -> {projects:[{id, name, instructions, updatedAt:iso, conversationCount, fileCount, coverUrl:string|null (file named '__cover__')}]} ordered updatedAt desc. POST /api/projects body {name?:1..120, instructions?:<=20000} -> 201 {id:cuid} (unnamed => 'Untitled project', nameSource 'default'). GET /api/projects/{id} -> {project:{id,name,instructions,updatedAt:iso}, conversations:[{id,title,pinned,lastMessageAt:iso}] (desc lastMessageAt), files: ClientAttachment[]}. PATCH /api/projects/{id} body {name?:1..120 (sets nameSource 'manual'), instructions?:<=20000} -> {ok:true}. DELETE /api/projects/{id} -> {ok:true} (conversations survive with projectId null; project files cascade-delete).

GET /api/memory?q=<substring> -> {memories:[{id, content, source:'AUTO'|'MANUAL', kind:'FACT'|'SUPPRESSION', sourceRef:string|null, createdAt:iso}] desc createdAt, summary:{content, updatedAt:iso, entryCount}|null}. POST /api/memory body {content:1..500} -> 201 {memory:{id,content,source:'MANUAL',createdAt:iso}} (no kind/sourceRef in create response). DELETE /api/memory -> {ok:true} (wipes entries+summary, marks all conversations processed so backfill won't re-learn). PATCH /api/memory/{id} body {content:1..500} -> {ok:true}. DELETE /api/memory/{id} -> {ok:true}.

GET /api/memory/backfill -> {remaining:number}. POST /api/memory/backfill (no body) -> {processedConversations, created, remaining} — processes max 2 conversations per call, loop while remaining>0; 503 {"error":"No model provider is configured."}.

POST /api/memory/consolidate (no body) -> {summary:{content,updatedAt:iso,entryCount}} or {summary:null} when no sources; 503 no provider; 502 {"error":"Couldn’t generate a summary right now — try again in a moment."}.

POST /api/memory/edit body {instruction:1..600} -> {proposal:{summary, operations:[{op:'add',content,suppress?:true}|{op:'update',id,before,content}|{op:'remove',id,before}]}} OR {refusal:string}; 503 no provider; 502 on provider failure (message distinguishes rate-limited vs out-of-credits). POST /api/memory/edit/apply body {operations:[same shapes]:1..8, update/remove need id+before, one op per id} -> {memories:[...as GET], summary:{...}|null, inverse:Operation[] (reversed undo sequence)}; 409 {"error":"Your memory changed since this edit was drafted. Delete it and ask again."} when any `before` is stale.

GET /api/prompts -> {prompts:[{id,title,body,useCount,createdAt:iso,updatedAt:iso}]} desc updatedAt, take 200 (MAX_PROMPTS). POST /api/prompts body {title:1..80, body:1..10000} -> 201 {prompt:{...}}; 409 at 200-prompt cap {"error":"You've saved the maximum of 200 prompts. Delete one to make room."}; rate 40/hr. PATCH /api/prompts/{id} body {title?|body?, at least one} -> {prompt:{...}}. DELETE /api/prompts/{id} -> {ok:true}. POST /api/prompts/{id} (use-count bump, fire-and-forget) -> {ok:true}; rate 240/hr; bump refreshes updatedAt (used prompt floats to top).

PATCH /api/settings body (all optional) {theme:'light'|'dark'|'system', accent:/^([a-z]+|#[0-9a-fA-F]{6})$/ (named ids: coral|teal|violet|amber|sage), defaultModel:<must pass isModelId, else 400 'Unknown model'>, personality:'default'|'concise'|'encouraging'|'socratic'|'formal'|'nerdy', customInstructions:<=4000, responseLanguage:<=40, uiLocale:<=35 ('auto' or normalizable tag, else 400 'Unknown locale'), memoryEnabled:bool, voiceId:string<=100|null, favoriteModels:string[]<=200, emailBudgetAlerts:bool, emailWeeklyDigest:bool} -> {ok:true}. NOTE: there is NO GET /api/settings — the web reads settings from server-rendered bootstrap; native clients read them via /api/v1/bootstrap.

DELETE /api/account (no body) -> {ok:true} — permanent delete, no confirmation. POST /api/account/delete body {confirmEmail} (must case-insensitively equal account email, else 400 {"error":"The email you typed doesn't match this account."}) -> {ok:true}; rate 3/hr -> 429.

GET /api/account/export -> full JSON snapshot as attachment (Content-Disposition juno-export-YYYY-MM-DD.json): {exportedAt, profile:{name,email,createdAt,plan}, settings, memories, memorySummary, projects, attachments:{note,items}, apiSpend:{requestCount,promptTokens,completionTokens,totalCostUsd,byKind:[{kind,requestCount,costUsd}]}, messagesTruncated:bool, truncationNote?, conversations:[{title,model,createdAt,updatedAt,messages:[{role,content,reasoning,model,promptTokens,completionTokens,createdAt}]}]}. ?format=csv -> text/csv (columns conversation,role,model,content,createdAt; CSV-injection neutralized). Cap 50,000 message rows; rate 5/hr.

POST /api/profile/avatar multipart form field `file` (image/jpeg|png|webp|gif, <=5MB) -> {url:"/api/files/<key>"}; errors 400 {"error":"No file provided."|"Please upload a JPEG, PNG, WebP, or GIF."|"Image must be under 5 MB."}; rate 20/hr. DELETE /api/profile/avatar -> {ok:true}.

GET /api/profile/stats -> {daily:{'YYYY-MM-DD':{tokens,count}}, models:[{model,count,tokens}] desc count, totalTokens, totalMessages, memberSince:iso|null} — last 371 days, max 50000 messages scanned.

GET /api/profile/usage -> {quota:{plan:'FREE'|'PRO'|'MAX'|'MAX20'|'OWNER', used, limit:number|null, remaining:number|null}, spend:{spentMicroUsd, budgetMicroUsd:number|null, remainingMicroUsd, eurPerUsd, windows:{session:{spentMicroUsd,budgetMicroUsd,pct,resetsAtMs},weekly:{same}}, billing:{renewsAtMs, cancelAtPeriodEnd:bool}}} — session = rolling 5h, weekly = rolling 7d, pct is fraction (>=1 = exhausted).

GET /api/models -> {models: ModelInfo[] (sorted lab asc, intelligence desc, released desc, name asc — trust order verbatim), manifestVersion:'v1-<16 hex>'}. ModelInfo per src/lib/models.ts: {id:'provider:providerModel', provider, providerModel, name, description?, minPlan, vision, reasoning, cost:1|2|3, modality:'chat'|'image'|'video', webSearch, status?:'current'|'legacy'|'deprecated', family?, deprecationNote?, contextWindow?, legacy?}. (Full native v1 catalog shape lives in nativeModelCatalog(), src/lib/model-catalog-api.ts, served via /api/v1/models.)

GET /api/announcements -> {announcement: {id,title,description,imageUrl,videoUrl,provider:Provider|null,modelName,newsLabel,newsHref,ctaLabel,ctaHref,published,startsAt:iso,endsAt:iso|null,createdAt:iso,updatedAt:iso} | null} — newest active non-dismissed; returns {announcement:null} even when UNAUTHENTICATED (no 401). POST /api/announcements/{id}/dismiss -> {ok:true} (upsert; 404 if announcement id unknown).

GET /api/share -> {shares:[{id, kind:'CHAT'|'ARTIFACT', token, url:'<appUrl>/share/<token>', title, snapshotAt:iso, views, createdAt:iso}]}. POST /api/share body {kind:'CHAT'|'ARTIFACT', conversationId?:cuid (CHAT), artifactId?:cuid (ARTIFACT)} -> {share:{...}} — reuses newest active link per target; snapshot frozen at creation. DELETE /api/share/{id} -> {ok:true} (revocation tombstone).

POST /api/import multipart form field `file` = ChatGPT/Claude export ZIP, <=100MB -> {imported:number, skipped:number, format:string}; 400 on unreadable ZIP / missing conversations.json; idempotent by (title,createdAt); rate 10/hr.

GET /api/library?kind=IMAGE|FILE -> {items:[ClientAttachment & {createdAt:iso, conversationId:string|null}]} desc createdAt, take 300. POST /api/library/attach body {attachmentIds:[cuid]1..10} -> 201 {attachments: ClientAttachment[]} — clones rows sharing the same storageKey (messageId/conversationId/projectId null) for reuse in a new message; 404 {"error":"No matching files."}.

POST /api/upload multipart form fields: `file` (required), `conversationId`? (ownership-checked, 404 'Conversation not found.'), `projectId`? (404 'Project not found.') -> 201 {attachment: ClientAttachment}. Accepted MIME: images image/png|jpeg|webp|gif (magic-byte verified — 415 "That file isn't a valid image." on mismatch); docs application/pdf, text/plain, text/markdown, text/csv, application/json, application/javascript, text/javascript, application/xml, text/xml, application/x-yaml, text/yaml, any text/*, application/octet-stream. Blocked: text/html, application/xhtml+xml, image/svg+xml -> 415 {"error":"Unsupported file type: <mime>."}. Size limit by plan maxUploadMb: FREE 5 / PRO 20 / MAX 50 / MAX20 50 / OWNER 1000 -> 413 {"error":"File is too large. Your plan allows up to N MB."}. 503 when no storage configured; rate 60/hr -> 429. Max attachments per message MAX_ATTACHMENTS=10. Text files <1MB get extractedText (first 200k chars) server-side.

GET /api/attachments/{id} -> raw image bytes (IMAGE kind only; Content-Type sniffed, Cache-Control private,max-age=300, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff); 404/415. PATCH /api/attachments/{id} body {fileName:1..200} -> {ok:true, fileName:<sanitized>} (display rename only; sanitizeFileName strips to [a-zA-Z0-9._ -], 120 chars). DELETE /api/attachments/{id} -> {ok:true} — deletes row, deletes storage object only when no other row references the same storageKey (library clones share objects).

GET /api/files/{...key} — NO auth check; serves locally-stored objects (dev fallback + avatar URLs). Key must start with 'uploads/'; images/videos inline (sniffed Content-Type, video supports HTTP Range/206 for playback), everything else forced attachment application/octet-stream. Security model = unguessable UUID in key.

GET /api/artifacts -> {items:[{id, identifier, title, type:'HTML'|'REACT'|'CODE'|'MARKDOWN'|'SVG'|'MERMAID', language:string|null, version:<currentVersion>, conversationId, conversationTitle, updatedAt:iso}]} desc updatedAt, take 200 (note: list field is `version`, not `currentVersion`). POST /api/artifacts/{id} body {content:<=200000} -> {artifact: ClientArtifact} — manual edit saved as NEW version (currentVersion+1). ClientArtifact = {id, identifier, type, title, language, currentVersion, content:<latest version text>, versions:[{version, content, createdAt:iso}] asc, messageId:string|null, createdAt:iso, updatedAt:iso}. Artifact content is plain text of its type (markdown / html / jsx / svg / mermaid source); no binary.

GET /api/artifacts/{id}/export (no `format` param) -> {formats:['docx'|'xlsx'|'pptx',...]} (empty unless type==='MARKDOWN'; Cache-Control no-store; rate 400/hr). GET /api/artifacts/{id}/export?format=docx|xlsx|pptx -> binary Office file (Content-Type per format, Content-Disposition attachment with ASCII filename + RFC5987 filename*; rate 60/hr); 400 {"error":"This artifact can’t be exported as <fmt>."} / {"error":"Invalid format"}; 500 {"error":"Could not build the file."}.
behaviors: Auth replay: persist the Auth.js session cookie (HttpOnly JWT, ~30-day maxAge, no refresh endpoint for credentials) and resend on every call; on any 401 re-run csrf->credentials-callback sign-in once and retry — this is the Swift app's rule. A Tauri client can instead use the native Bearer token flow (/api/v1/auth/*) since getCurrentUser accepts Authorization: Bearer on every route.

Conversation create-then-stream: the Swift app POSTs /api/conversations (with kind:'chat'|'code' and codeWorkspace* for Code sessions) to get a canonical cuid BEFORE first send, and retro-PATCHes kind/codeWorkspace on already-synced sessions. It calls POST /api/conversations/{id}/title with {phase:'completed'} after the first exchange and displays whatever title comes back (server may decline: renamed:false).

Archive semantics: client sends PATCH {archived:true|false}; server stores archivedAt timestamp and only stamps it on the first archive (re-PATCHing an archived chat must not move the timestamp). Default list view must exclude archived conversations (server already does).
Search is title-only: q param cannot match message bodies (encrypted at rest) — replicate by not promising full-text search, or search locally over synced plaintext.

Ordering rules to replicate verbatim: conversations pinned-first then lastMessageAt desc; projects updatedAt desc; prompts updatedAt desc with use-bump floating a just-used prompt to top (POST /api/prompts/{id} fired without awaiting, failures swallowed); library createdAt desc; artifacts updatedAt desc; models list order trusted verbatim for the picker.

Memory NL editing is a two-phase optimistic flow: POST /api/memory/edit renders a reviewable diff (before/after per op); on Accept POST /api/memory/edit/apply and keep the returned `inverse` array to offer one-click Undo (apply the inverse via the same endpoint); a 409 means stale draft — discard and re-ask.
Memory reset (DELETE /api/memory) must be presented as permanent: it also marks all conversations processed so backfill never re-learns them.
Backfill is client-driven polling: GET remaining, then POST in a loop while remaining>0 (2 conversations per call), showing progress; resumable at any point.

Settings sync is push-only PATCH with sparse bodies (send only changed keys); the app mirrors customInstructions/responseLanguage so server-side system prompts stay in lockstep. Reads come from bootstrap, not a GET /api/settings.

Upload flow: direct multipart POST to /api/upload BEFORE sending the chat message, then pass returned attachment ids as attachmentIds in POST /api/chat (max 10). Attaching an existing library file goes through POST /api/library/attach to clone a fresh unlinked row — never reuse the original attachment id.
Attachment delete is safe against shared storage: server refcounts storageKey; client just deletes the row and refreshes.

Rate-limit handling: expect 429 with a human message in `error` on title (20/60s), prompts create (40/hr) and use (240/hr), upload (60/hr), avatar (20/hr), export (5/hr), import (10/hr), account delete (3/hr), artifact export (60/hr gen, 400/hr detect) — back off and surface `error` text; owner account is exempt.
Fire-and-forget endpoints (must never surface failures): POST /api/prompts/{id} use-bump, POST /api/announcements/{id}/dismiss.
Announcement banner: GET /api/announcements on launch; show if non-null; POST dismiss on close; endpoint returns {announcement:null} rather than 401 when signed out.

Usage meters: refresh GET /api/profile/usage on app foreground and after any HTTP 402 from chat/agent endpoints; render session (5h) and weekly windows from pct with resetsAtMs countdowns; budgets are micro-USD, converted to EUR for display via eurPerUsd.
Avatar update: multipart POST then immediately re-fetch profile (GET /api/auth/session or bootstrap) so the returned /api/files/... URL renders; DELETE clears to null.

Artifact editing: saving user edits POSTs full content and creates a new version — clients should render `versions` for a version pager and treat `content` as the latest. Export UI: call export without `format` first and only offer the returned formats.
Fork ('branch from here'): POST fork with the anchor message id, then navigate to the returned conversation; do not expect attachments/artifacts/version history to carry over.

risks: BACKEND_API.md is stale in specific places: (1) it claims 'No bearer tokens' but src/lib/session.ts now authenticates `Authorization: Bearer` native device tokens (and an invalid Bearer returns 401 without cookie fallback — never send a junk Authorization header); (2) it says conversations `q` searches message content — messages are AES-GCM encrypted at rest and search is title-only; (3) ClientConversation gained titleSource, kind, codeWorkspaceName/Path, activeConnectors, archivedAt; (4) /api/models response gained manifestVersion; (5) POST /api/conversations now takes a body. Do not code the Windows client from the doc alone.

No pagination or cursors anywhere: conversations cap at 200 (a heavy account silently truncates the sidebar), library 300, prompts 200, artifacts 200, export 50k message rows. The Windows client should prefer the /api/v1 delta-sync surface (bootstrap/changes/mutations) for full history rather than these capped lists.

GET /api/files/{...key} has NO auth — capability-URL security only (UUID keys). Avatar URLs are /api/files/... and load without cookies; but with S3 configured, attachment `url` values from serializeAttachment may be 1-hour presigned URLs that EXPIRE — never cache them long-term, re-fetch the owning object to refresh.

Two different image-bytes paths: /api/attachments/{id} (authed, IMAGE-only, 5-min cache) vs the url field (proxy or S3). For a desktop webview that can't attach cookies to , use the url field; use /api/attachments/{id} when you need authenticated same-origin bytes.

Upload MIME rules are enforced server-side by declared MIME except images (magic-byte sniffed). text/html, xhtml, svg are hard-blocked (XSS) — don't offer them in the picker. Non-image files are stored as application/octet-stream and force-download.
Rate limits are per-user and invisible until 429; the owner email (OWNER_EMAILS env) bypasses them, so testing against an owner account hides throttling bugs.

DELETE /api/conversations (bulk wipe) and DELETE /api/account are unconfirmed single calls — gate them behind explicit UI confirmation; POST /api/account/delete is the safer email-confirming variant the web UI uses.
PATCH /api/settings validates defaultModel against the server's live model registry (isModelId) — a model id cached client-side can 400 as 'Unknown model' after server catalog changes.

There is no GET /api/settings, /api/account (profile) GET, or per-conversation message pagination — thread loads are all-or-nothing GET /api/conversations/{id}, which can be large; plan for streaming JSON parse or v1 sync.
Conversation PATCH archived semantics: `archived` is a boolean in the request but `archivedAt` (timestamp|null) in responses — model this asymmetry explicitly.

Folders GET createdAt is a raw Date serialized by NextResponse.json (ISO string) while other routes call .toISOString() explicitly — same wire format, but the POST /api/folders 201 response nests {folder:{id,name,createdAt}} while PATCH returns only {folder:{id,name}}.
memory POST response omits kind/sourceRef; the list response includes them — normalize client-side.
Artifact export formats are only available for MARKDOWN artifacts and detection is rate-limited separately (400/hr) from generation (60/hr); cache the formats response per artifact version.
Import (POST /api/import) has maxDuration=300 on Vercel only; self-hosted `next start` ignores it — long ZIP imports may exceed default timeouts on other deployments.

Env assumptions: storage endpoints 503 when no bucket configured (isStorageAvailable); memory/title/edit endpoints 503 when no LLM provider key configured — the Windows client must handle 503 as 'feature unavailable', not fatal.

summary: Juno has TWO voice systems. (1) REALTIME VOICE ("Juno Voice"): a standalone Node WebSocket relay (juno/relay/, PM2 as juno-voice-relay on :8787, nginx-proxied at wss://chat.liams.dev/voice-relay) that hides all provider keys. Clients (web hook src/hooks/use-realtime-voice.ts, iOS/macOS RealtimeVoiceController.swift) open ONE WebSocket: binary frames carry mic PCM16LE mono 16 kHz up and model speech PCM16LE mono 24 kHz down; JSON text frames carry control/transcript/usage events defined in relay/src/protocol.ts (mirrored in src/lib/voice-relay-protocol.ts and VoiceRelayProtocol.swift — "change all three together"). Auth is a 60-second HMAC token minted by GET /api/voice/relay-token (signState over {"uid","exp"} with the shared AUTH_SECRET; relay verifies it with no DB access). Providers: openai (GA Realtime WS, gpt-realtime-2.1), gemini (Live API WS with transparent goAway/resumption-handle reconnect), qwen (DashScope OpenAI-Realtime beta dialect), minimax (honest composed pipeline: CLIENT on-device speech recognition -> input.text -> streaming chat completion -> sentence-chunked T2A WebSocket TTS, hex-encoded PCM 24k), plus dev-only mock (RELAY_ENABLE_MOCK=1). The relay owns the running finalized transcript so mid-session provider switches re-seed context (last 30 turns) into the new provider; finalized turns are persisted into a normal chat conversation by the CLIENT via idempotent POST /api/voice/transcript (sessionId-keyed, sendBeacon fallback on unload). Barge-in: client sends control.interrupt and flushes local playback; relay/providers cancel output and emit "interrupted". Usage estimates are pushed every 5 s. (2) LEGACY/READ-ALOUD VOICE: voice-mode.tsx loop (listen -> POST /api/chat with voiceMode:true -> POST /api/voice/tts mp3 playback), plus dictation via POST /api/voice/stt and POST /api/voice/asr — all degrade silently to browser Web Speech APIs when unconfigured.
contracts: GET /api/voice/relay-token (juno/src/app/api/voice/relay-token/route.ts, cookie-auth) -> 200 {"token": string, "url": string, "providers"?: {[providerId]: boolean}}. token = base64url(JSON '{"uid":<userId>,"exp":<epochSec now+60>}') + "." + HMAC-SHA256(base64url, key=AUTH_SECRET) — same signState as src/lib/crypto.ts; relay verifies in relay/src/auth.ts (timingSafeEqual, exp check). url from NEXT_PUBLIC_VOICE_RELAY_URL || VOICE_RELAY_URL. providers is best-effort copy of relay GET /healthz body.providers (1500 ms timeout; omitted on failure). Errors: 401 {error:"Unauthorized"}; 403 {error:"Voice mode requires a paid plan."} (PLANS[plan].voice false); 429 {error:"Slow down."} (rateLimit key relay-token:<uid>, 30/60s, skipped for owner email); 402 {error:"budget_exceeded", message:<human copy>}; 503 {error:"Realtime voice is not configured."}

Relay WebSocket connect: <relayUrl>/?token=<token> (query param 'token'). Upgrade rejected with raw 'HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized' (bad/expired token) or 403 (Origin present and not in ALLOWED_ORIGINS; requests WITHOUT an Origin header always pass — native apps). ws maxPayload 4 MiB (relay/src/server.ts).
GET <relay http url>/healthz -> {"ok":true, "providers":{"openai":bool,"gemini":bool,"qwen":bool,"minimax":bool,"mock":bool}}; path suffix-matched so /voice-relay/healthz works behind the proxy.

Relay wire protocol (relay/src/protocol.ts — SOURCE OF TRUTH; mirrors src/lib/voice-relay-protocol.ts, VoiceRelayProtocol.swift). BINARY client->relay: mic audio PCM16 little-endian mono 16000 Hz (MIC_SAMPLE_RATE). BINARY relay->client: model speech PCM16LE mono 24000 Hz (PLAYBACK_SAMPLE_RATE; relay resamples any provider rate to 24k). TEXT frames: one JSON message per frame.

Client->relay JSON messages: {"type":"session.start","provider":"openai"|"gemini"|"qwen"|"minimax"|"mock","history"?:[{"role":"user"|"assistant","text":string}]} (history honored only on the FIRST session.start; later starts/switches reuse the relay's own finalized transcript); {"type":"session.switch","provider":...}; {"type":"input.text","text":string,"turnId"?:string,"displayText"?:string} (text+displayText trimmed and capped at 4000 chars server-side; relay echoes it back as a final user transcript with the same turnId, and forwards text to the provider); {"type":"control.interrupt"}; {"type":"video.frame","jpegBase64":string} (base64 without data: prefix, decoded JPEG must be >0 and <2,000,000 bytes, send <=1 fps); {"type":"ping"}.

Relay->client JSON messages: {"type":"session.ready","provider":VoiceProviderId,"capabilities":{"videoInput":bool,"screenInput"?:bool,"trueS2S":bool,"needsClientTranscript":bool,"maxSessionSec":number}}; {"type":"transcript","role":"user"|"assistant","text":string,"final":bool,"turnId"?:string} — partials are DELTAS to append; a final with non-empty text REPLACES the accumulated partial; a final with empty text (Gemini commit marker) just SEALS the partial; {"type":"turn","speaker":"assistant","phase":"start"|"end"}; {"type":"interrupted"} (client MUST flush its audio playback queue immediately and seal the assistant partial); {"type":"usage","provider":id,"audioInSec":number(1dp),"audioOutSec":number(1dp),"estCostUsd":number(6dp),"estCostInUsd"?:number,"estCostOutUsd"?:number} pushed every 5 s (in/out split omitted — not zero — when a side was never measured); {"type":"session.closed","reason":"session-limit"|"provider"|"client"|"error"}; {"type":"error","message":string}; {"type":"pong"}.

History bounds (enforced client-side AND at the relay trust boundary): VOICE_HISTORY_MAX_TURNS=20, VOICE_HISTORY_MAX_TURN_CHARS=2000, VOICE_HISTORY_MAX_TOTAL_CHARS=12000; newest-first trimming so the latest turns survive. Provider seeding uses relay transcript.slice(-30).

Provider capabilities/limits (relay/src/providers/registry.ts): openai {videoInput:true, screenInput:false, trueS2S:true, needsClientTranscript:false, maxSessionSec:3600}; gemini {true, true, true, false, 900}; qwen {true, true, true, false, 7200}; minimax {false, false, trueS2S:false, needsClientTranscript:true, 7200}; mock {true, true, true, false, 3600}. Relay enforces maxSessionSec with a timer -> session.closed reason "session-limit". Default voices: openai "marin", qwen "Ethan", minimax "English_expressive_narrator", gemini provider default. System prompt VOICE_INSTRUCTIONS lives server-side in relay/src/session.ts and is never sent to clients.

POST /api/voice/transcript (juno/src/app/api/voice/transcript/route.ts) body: {"sessionId": uuid, "conversationId"?: cuid|null, "model": string<=120, "projectId"?: cuid|null, "connectors"?: string[]<=5 each<=120, "turns": [{"role":"USER"|"ASSISTANT", "content": string 1..20000, "attachmentIds": cuid[]<=4 (default [])}] min 1 max 1000} -> 200 {"conversationId": string, "messages": serializedMessage[]}. IDEMPOTENT on (userId, sessionId) via voiceTranscriptSession table — retries and sendBeacon duplicates return the saved result. Creates the conversation if conversationId null (title = first USER turn slice(0,48), titleSource "default", activeConnectors from connectors); messages get monotonic backfilled createdAt ending at now; attachments must be user-owned kind IMAGE with messageId null. Errors: 400 {error:"Invalid voice transcript"}; 403 plan; 404 {error:"Conversation not found"}; 409 {error:"One or more voice images are unavailable."|"A voice image was already used."}; 429 (30/3600s); 500 {error:"Could not save the voice transcript."}.

POST /api/voice/tts body {"text": string 1..4000, "voiceId"?: string<=100} -> 200 raw audio bytes, headers Content-Type: audio/mpeg, Cache-Control: no-store. 501 {error:"Server TTS not configured."} => client falls back to window.speechSynthesis. 401/403/429 (120/60s)/400/502 {error:"Text-to-speech failed."}. Server chain: TTS_PROVIDER order (openai gpt-4o-mini-tts default via env TTS_MODEL, voice default TTS_VOICE 'alloy', response_format mp3, plus an 'instructions' string for /^gpt-4o.*-tts$/ models telling it to read in the text's own language; ElevenLabs fallback model_id eleven_multilingual_v2, header xi-api-key, default voice 21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM). Valid OpenAI voiceIds (src/lib/voices.ts VOICE_IDS): alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer, coral, verse, ballad, ash, sage, marin, cedar; non-OpenAI ids are passed to ElevenLabs.

POST /api/voice/stt multipart form-data {audio: File (<=25 MB), language?: string (normalized to 2-letter ISO-639-1)} -> 200 {"text": string}. 501 {error:"Server STT not configured."} => browser SpeechRecognition fallback. 413 {error:"That clip is too long."}; 502 {error:"Transcription failed."}. Providers: STT_PROVIDER=openai -> /v1/audio/transcriptions model STT_MODEL (default gpt-4o-transcribe, auto-retry whisper-1), response_format json, filename extension matters; or deepgram nova-3 (query smart_format/punctuate/language, header Authorization: Token <key>).

POST /api/voice/asr multipart {audio: File, model: string default "zhipu:glm-asr-2512"} -> {"text": string}. Resolves against VOICE_INPUT_MODELS (src/lib/models.ts): "zhipu:glm-asr-2512" (provider zhipu, providerModel glm-asr-2512) and "browser" (on-device, rejected here with 400). Uses the provider's OpenAI-compatible /audio/transcriptions. 501 when provider unconfigured; 502 with providerErrorMessage.

POST /api/chat with {"voiceMode": true} (legacy voice loop): disables deepResearch and canvas, appends a '# Voice mode' system-prompt section (concise, no Markdown; src/lib/anthropic.ts). Streamed via the normal chat stream (meta/delta/error chunks read by readChatStream).

Upstream provider dialects held by the relay (needed only if reimplementing the relay, not the client): OpenAI GA realtime wss://api.openai.com/v1/realtime?model=<RELAY_OPENAI_MODEL|gpt-realtime-2.1>, Authorization: Bearer; session.update {type:"realtime", instructions, audio:{input:{format:{type:"audio/pcm",rate:24000}, turn_detection:{type:"server_vad",create_response:true,interrupt_response:true}, transcription:{model:"gpt-realtime-whisper"}}, output:{format:{...24000}, voice:"marin"}}}; events handled: response.output_audio.delta|response.audio.delta (base64 pcm), response.[output_]audio_transcript.delta/.done, conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.delta/.completed, input_audio_buffer.speech_started (auto barge-in), response.done (usage tokens), error. Qwen: wss://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api-ws/v1/realtime?model=<qwen3.5-omni-flash-realtime>, headers Authorization Bearer + OpenAI-Beta: realtime=v1, flat beta session {modalities:["text","audio"], voice:"Ethan", input_audio_format:"pcm16", output_audio_format:"pcm16", input_audio_transcription:{model:"gummy-realtime-v1"}, turn_detection:{type:"semantic_vad"}}, input resampled to 16k, video via input_image_buffer.append. Gemini Live: wss://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/ws/google.ai.generativelanguage.v1beta.GenerativeService.BidiGenerateContent, header x-goog-api-key (classic AIza… key required); setup {model:"models/<gemini-3.1-flash-live-preview>", generationConfig:{responseModalities:["AUDIO"]}, systemInstruction, inputAudioTranscription:{}, outputAudioTranscription:{}, contextWindowCompression:{slidingWindow:{}}, sessionResumption:{handle?}}; audio up {realtimeInput:{audio:{data:b64,mimeType:"audio/pcm;rate=16000"}}}; handles goAway -> transparent reconnect with resumption handle. MiniMax: streaming POST <MINIMAX_BASE_URL|https://api.minimax.io/v1>/chat/completions (model MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed, max_tokens 400) + TTS WS wss://api.minimax.io/ws/v1/t2a_v2 events task_start {model:"speech-2.6-turbo", voice_setting:{voice_id,speed:1,vol:1,pitch:0}, audio_setting:{format:"pcm",sample_rate:24000,channel:1}} / task_continue {text} / task_finish; replies task_started|connected_success, task_continued {data.audio: HEX-encoded PCM, is_final}.
behaviors: Session start sequence (web, use-realtime-voice.ts start()): GET /api/voice/relay-token -> apply providers availability (force minimax=false when the platform has no speech recognition; if requested provider is explicitly false, fall back to first available, else error 'No compatible realtime voice provider is available in this browser.') -> create playback AudioContext({sampleRate:24000}) -> getUserMedia({audio:{echoCancellation:true,noiseSuppression:true,autoGainControl:true}}) + AudioWorklet mic tap -> open WS <url>/?token=... (binaryType arraybuffer) -> on open send session.start with provider + bounded history (initial chat context + any finalized voice lines) -> status 'connecting' until session.ready flips to 'live'. Generation counter guards every async step against superseded starts.

Mic upload: worklet Float32 chunks are RMS-metered (level = min(1, rms*5)) then, ONLY when neither muted nor assistantSpeaking, downsampled from the context rate to 16 kHz by decimation-average into Int16 and sent as one binary WS frame per chunk. HALF-DUPLEX IS MANDATORY: playback goes through a separate audio graph the OS echo-canceller cannot see, so uploading while the model speaks makes the model transcribe and answer itself. There is no talk-over barge-in; users interrupt via the on-screen Stop control (control.interrupt).

Playback: each binary frame is Int16->Float32, scheduled on a 24 kHz AudioContext with a rolling cursor (start at max(now+0.04, cursor)); sources tracked in a Set. assistantSpeaking turns on at first scheduled buffer or turn.start, and only turns OFF when turn phase 'end' has arrived AND the scheduled queue has fully drained (turn.end often beats the audio). 'interrupted' => stop all sources, reset cursor, seal the assistant's partial transcript line, clear speaking flags.

Transcript rendering (pushTranscript): keep an ordered line list; a partial appends its text to the last non-final line of the same role (creating one if absent); a final with text replaces that line's text; a final with empty text seals it. Search by role, not last row — user input transcription can finalize after the assistant already started streaming. Typed turns arrive echoed from the relay with turnId; turnId maps to locally-held image attachments so they render on that user line exactly once.

Provider switch (live only): client seals all partials, flushes playback, stops screen share and client-side speech recognition, sets status 'connecting', sends {type:'session.switch'}; the SAME WebSocket stays up; relay tears down the old provider, commits partials into its transcript, starts the new provider seeded with instructions + last 30 finalized turns, then sends session.ready. A providerEpoch counter prevents in-flight image conversions from landing in the new provider.

MiniMax (needsClientTranscript) mode: on session.ready with needsClientTranscript=true the client starts continuous on-device speech recognition (Web Speech / SFSpeechRecognizer with partials, dictation hint, punctuation); final utterances are sent as {type:'input.text'} ONLY when live, unmuted, and not assistantSpeaking; recognizer auto-restarts 250 ms after onend (300 ms throttle on iOS); interim text is displayed locally as a streaming user line (id 'voice-speech-interim'); recognition errors surface as 'Browser speech recognition stopped (<err>). Switch voice models or restart voice.' Mute toggling starts/stops the recognizer too.

Typed/composer turns during voice (sendTurn): rejected unless live; only IMAGE attachments, max 4; images fetched via authed GET /api/attachments/{id}, bitmap-downscaled to max side 1280, JPEG quality ladder 0.8/0.68/0.54/0.42 until decoded size <=1,900,000 bytes, each sent as video.frame; then input.text with turnId=crypto.randomUUID(), text = typed text or 'Please look at the image context I just shared and respond naturally.', displayText = typed text or 'Shared an image'/'Shared N images'. Providers without videoInput reject image turns client-side with a toast suggesting OpenAI/Gemini/Qwen.

Screen share (capabilities.screenInput only — gemini/qwen/mock): getDisplayMedia({video:{frameRate:2},audio:false}); 1 fps setInterval captures the video element to canvas scaled to width<=1024, JPEG 0.6, sends video.frame; auto-stops when the track ends, the provider switches, or capabilities drop. iOS uses ReplayKit at the same 1 fps/1024px/0.6 parameters; gated on videoInput there (Swift capabilities struct predates screenInput).

Persistence of a finished session (chat-view.tsx closeVoice): a sessionId (uuid) is minted when voice OPENS; on End, finalized non-empty lines become turns [{role USER/ASSISTANT, content, attachmentIds}] and POST /api/voice/transcript (fetch keepalive when payload <=60,000 bytes); on success the returned messages are appended to the chat (deduped by id), a conversation is upserted/navigated to if new, and the transcript clears. On failure the payload is retained for retry/discard, with a toast. Unmount/new-chat fires navigator.sendBeacon('/api/voice/transcript', Blob(payload, type application/json)) with the SAME sessionId — the endpoint's idempotency makes double-saves safe. Private (incognito) mode skips saving entirely.

History seeding on open: current chat's USER/ASSISTANT messages with non-empty content map to {role:'user'|'assistant', text}, bounded to 20 turns / 2000 chars/turn / 12000 total (newest-first) before session.start. The relay accepts history once per connection.

Usage display: 'usage' events every 5 s update a '~$X' pill (formatUsd, 4dp render); tooltip splits 'you ~$in · Juno ~$out' only when BOTH estCostInUsd and estCostOutUsd are present. No aria-live on cost.

Orb/level meter: single smoothed 0..1 level ref, rAF lerp level += (target-level)*0.25; target = playback RMS*4 (capped, decaying *0.92/frame — Swift 30 Hz, *0.86, playback*2.5, mic*4) while speaking, else mic RMS, else 0 when muted. Orb states: error / thinking(connecting) / idle(muted or not live) / speaking / listening.

Status machine (web): idle -> connecting -> live -> ended|error; 'ended' or 'error' shows a Restart button that re-runs start(). session.closed with reason !== 'client' => 'ended' with reason retained (closedReason). Relay 'error' messages while connecting set status 'error'; while live they surface as a message but the session continues (Swift shows a 6 s transient notice).

Reconnect semantics: WEB DOES NOT RECONNECT — any ws close seals the transcript, releases resources, status 'ended'. SWIFT attempts EXACTLY ONE reconnect when the socket fails while live: phase 'reconnecting', 1 s delay, refetch token, new WS, re-send session.start with initialHistory + sealed voice turns (relay-side this is a NEW connection so history is accepted again); second failure => error state. The RELAY internally rides Gemini's ~10-min connection cycling via sessionResumption handles, invisible to clients. Keepalive: Swift pings every 20 s ({type:'ping'}->pong); web sends no pings.

Barge-in flows: user presses interrupt -> client sends control.interrupt + flushes playback immediately (does not wait for the relay). OpenAI/Qwen also auto-cancel server-side on input_audio_buffer.speech_started -> relay emits turn end + interrupted. Gemini: sc.interrupted from its VAD, or relay-side output suppression after manual interrupt (no cancel frame exists). MiniMax: interrupt aborts the LLM stream, clears the sentence queue, restarts the TTS socket (task_finish + close + reopen). Every path ends with the 'interrupted' event so clients flush and seal.
Mute: gates BOTH the PCM upload and the client-side recognizer, mic level still metered for the orb; mic button disabled unless live.

Voice UI is a thin dock over the normal chat: transcript lines are mapped into ChatMessage objects (id 'voice-<n>', streaming=!final, voice:true) and appended after chat.messages; composer keeps working and routes through sendTurn while voice is open; opening voice is blocked while chat.isBusy, in private mode, or with an unsaved previous voice transcript.

Legacy VoiceMode loop (voice-mode.tsx / Swift VoiceSessionController): listening (browser Web Speech OR MediaRecorder->POST /api/voice/asr with model id) -> thinking (POST /api/chat {conversationId?, message, model, voiceMode:true}, stream deltas, cleanForSpeech) -> speaking (useTts: POST /api/voice/tts -> audio/mpeg blob -> <audio> with client-side playbackRate; browser speechSynthesis fallback) -> auto-resume listening. Timers ported everywhere: 60 ms initial start, 400 ms relisten after empty utterance, 1.5 s resume after chat error, 1.8 s after ASR error. Speed persisted in localStorage 'juno:voice:speed' (0.8/1/1.25/1.5/1.75/2). Esc closes. Voice choice PATCHes /api/settings {voiceId}.

Dictation (composer-dictation.tsx): Web Speech drives the live preview only; MediaRecorder records in parallel and the FINAL text is server-transcribed via /api/voice/stt with FormData audio + language=navigator.language; falls back to the Web Speech text when the route 501s/fails or the clip is <1200 bytes. Bootstrap features.serverStt/features.serverTts tell the UI whether server engines exist.

risks: Origin allowlist: the relay 403s any upgrade whose Origin header is present but not in ALLOWED_ORIGINS (prod: https://chat.liams.dev,http://localhost:3000). A Tauri WebView WebSocket sends an Origin like http://tauri.localhost (Windows) — either add it to ALLOWED_ORIGINS on the VM or open the socket from Rust where no Origin is sent (native apps 'send no Origin and always pass').

Relay token is valid for 60 seconds and single-purpose (connect window only); mint it immediately before opening the WS. Auth for all /api/voice/* routes is the Auth.js v5 session cookie (__Secure-authjs.session-token on https) — the Windows client must carry that cookie jar for token minting, tts/stt/asr, and transcript persistence.

Half-duplex is a hard requirement, not a web quirk: neither Web Audio in a WebView nor WASAPI gives echo cancellation across a separate playback graph, so uploading mic audio while assistant audio plays makes the model answer itself. Port the muted||assistantSpeaking upload gate and the 'speaking clears only after turn.end AND playback drain' bookkeeping exactly.

WebView2 has no Web Speech SpeechRecognition — the MiniMax provider (needsClientTranscript) cannot work without a native STT substitute (e.g. Windows.Media.SpeechRecognition bridged over Tauri IPC). Until then replicate the web guard: force availability.minimax=false when no recognizer exists.

Web client deliberately does NOT reconnect (session ends on socket loss); Swift does exactly one reconnect with token refetch and history re-seed. Pick one policy; naive infinite reconnect would re-bill provider sessions and can duplicate history seeding.

NEXT_PUBLIC_VOICE_RELAY_URL is build-time inlined in the web bundle, but the relay-token response's url field is authoritative at runtime — the Windows client should treat a configured relay URL only as a fallback (Swift: token url overrides BackendConfiguration.voiceRelayURL). Normalize http(s)->ws(s) and strip trailing slashes (normalizeRelayUrl).

Provider availability map may contain ids the client doesn't know (e.g. 'mock' in prod healthz) — ignore unknown keys (Swift does); only an EXPLICIT false disables a provider (absent map = assume available).

Plan/budget gating: PLANS[plan].voice must be true (free plan blocked, 403); non-owner users hit rate limits (relay-token 30/min, tts/stt/asr 120/min, transcript 30/hour) and a 402 budget_exceeded with human copy in 'message' not 'error'.
WS maxPayload is 4 MiB — keep video.frame base64 under it (client already caps decoded JPEG at ~1.9 MB) and never batch mic frames that large.

Transcript finals vs partials have three provider-specific shapes (delta partials + full-text final from OpenAI/Qwen/MiniMax; rolling partials + EMPTY-text final commit from Gemini; relay-echoed turnId finals for typed turns) — the pushTranscript merge algorithm must handle all three or lines duplicate/vanish.

sessionId idempotency is the only duplicate-save protection: mint one uuid per voice session at OPEN time and reuse it for every retry/beacon; a fresh uuid per attempt duplicates the whole conversation.
Session ceilings are enforced server-side (openai 60 min, gemini 15 min, qwen/minimax 120 min) -> session.closed 'session-limit'; the UI needs an ended-state with restart, not an error.

Provider account state (docs/voice.md, as of 2026-07-04): OpenAI quota exhausted, Gemini needs a classic AIza… key in GEMINI_LIVE_API_KEY (AQ.… keys rejected by the Live API), Qwen DashScope key invalid, MiniMax out of token-plan credit — for development use the mock provider (RELAY_ENABLE_MOCK=1) rather than debugging against dead upstreams.
GDPR: selecting qwen ships user audio to Alibaba Cloud (Singapore); the product expects a disclosure/consent notice when Qwen is picked.
Cost figures are ESTIMATES from relay list prices (usage.estCostUsd, 6dp; render ~4dp with a '~' prefix); show the in/out split only when both fields are present — absent means unmeasured, not $0.

Legacy voice endpoints degrade SILENTLY to browser APIs when TTS_PROVIDER/STT_PROVIDER are unset (501 responses are expected control flow, not errors); bootstrap features.serverStt/serverTts is how the client knows which engine it will actually get.

The Swift protocol file declares the wire format 'Frozen; do not extend unilaterally' — any Windows-driven protocol change must update relay/src/protocol.ts, src/lib/voice-relay-protocol.ts, VoiceRelayProtocol.swift, and the new client together. Note the Swift VoiceCapabilities struct omits screenInput (it ignores unknown JSON) — include it in the Windows type.
voiceMode:true on /api/chat suppresses canvas/deep-research and changes the system prompt — reuse it for any spoken-reply path so replies stay speakable.

summary: Juno chat streaming runs over a single POST /api/chat endpoint (Next.js route, src/app/api/chat/route.ts, self-hosted Node — no function timeout) that returns an SSE stream of JSON `data:` frames typed by a `type` field (meta/activity/sources/reasoning/delta/done/error/ping; `title` and `progress` exist in the union but chat never emits them — progress belongs to /api/generate, title to no one). Auth is either an `Authorization: Bearer` native JWT (10-min TTL, PKCE flow, audience "juno-native" — checked first, no cookie fallback on invalid bearer) or the next-auth `authjs.session-token` cookie. Generation is deliberately DETACHED from the HTTP request: dropping the connection does not stop it (the server persists and bills the full answer); the only way to stop is POST /api/chat/cancel with the client-minted generationId, which aborts an in-process AbortController registry. Two modes share the endpoint: persisted (conversationId cuid; server rebuilds history/memory/project context from DB, persists both turns, HISTORY_LIMIT 24 with 8-step chunked truncation for prompt-cache stability) and private (privateMode:true + inline privateHistory ≤24; nothing persisted, connectors/regenerate/deepResearch refused, meta.conversationId="private", done.message.id="private-<ts>"). Regenerate/edit never destroy history: the old answer/wording is snapshotted into an append-only MessageVersion and the Message row is overwritten IN PLACE (same id, bumped createdAt) — clients page old content via GET /api/messages/[id]/versions. Auto-title is fully client-driven (debounced POST /api/conversations/[id]/title with phases first_user/thinking/writing/completed/stopped). Preflight clarification (POST /api/chat/clarify, fail-open, 6s client timeout) and follow-up pills (POST /api/chat/follow-ups) are best-effort decorations. The Swift app's BackendChatTransport consumes the identical SSE frames but ignores activity/progress, builds its own timeline, sends client:"app", and inlines its local system prompt into private history.
contracts: POST /api/chat — request body (zod, unknown keys stripped): { conversationId?: cuid, projectId?: cuid (only honored when creating a new conversation), message?: string(≤50000), clarification?: { messageId: cuid, blockId: string(3-120), originalUserMessage: string(≤50000), answers: [{ id: string(1-80), question?: string(≤500), value?: string(≤1000)|string[](≤12×500)|boolean, skipped?: boolean }](≤10), skippedQuestions: string[](≤10×500) }, preflightClarification?: { originalUserMessage: string(≤50000), answers: [{ questionId: string(1-80), question?: string(≤500), source: "option"|"else"|"skip", value?: string|string[]|boolean }](≤10), skipped?: boolean }, attachmentIds?: cuid[](≤10, MAX_ATTACHMENTS), model?: string, regenerate?: boolean, voiceMode?: boolean, canvasEnabled?: boolean (server default true), webSearch?: boolean, deepResearch?: boolean, reasoningEffort?: "minimal"|"low"|"medium"|"high"|"xhigh"|"max", connectors?: string[](≤5), generationId?: string(8-120), privateMode?: boolean, client?: "web"|"app", privateHistory?: [{ role: "USER"|"ASSISTANT", content: string(≤50000) }](≤24) }. Non-regenerate requires message.trim() OR clarification OR ≥1 attachmentId, else 400 {error:"Message cannot be empty."}

POST /api/chat — response headers (SSE_HEADERS in src/lib/chat-stream.ts): Content-Type: text/event-stream; charset=utf-8, Cache-Control: no-cache, no-transform, Connection: keep-alive, X-Accel-Buffering: no. Every frame is `data: <json>

` (no event: names, no id:); client splits buffer on 

, strips leading "data:", JSON.parses, ignores malformed frames

SSE chunk union (src/types/chat.ts StreamChunk — literal): {type:"meta", conversationId, userMessageId: string|null, title, titleSource?: "default"|"ai"|"manual", generationId?} | {type:"title", conversationId, title, titleSource?} | {type:"activity", event:{id, kind, title, detail?, url?, createdAt}} | {type:"sources", sources: ClientSource[]} | {type:"reasoning", text, part?: number} | {type:"delta", text} | {type:"progress", stage:"queued"|"generating"|"polling"|"downloading"|"uploading", pct?, note?} | {type:"done", message: ClientMessage, artifacts: ClientArtifact[], memoryUpdated: boolean, quota: ClientQuota, finishReason?, title?, projectId?: string|null, projectName?: string|null} | {type:"error", message, quota?, finishReason?, preservePartial?} | {type:"ping"}

ActivityKind enum (activity.event.kind): "context"|"model"|"reasoning"|"search"|"visit"|"write"|"usage"|"done"|"warning"|"tool". Activity event ids are `activity-${Date.now()}-${counter}`; sources chunks resend the CUMULATIVE array each time it grows; ClientSource = {title, url, snippet, cited?} (cited=true only for deep-research corpus sources — inline [n] citation chips may only render when cited is set)
ChatFinishReason enum: "stop"|"length"|"network_error"|"model_context_window_exceeded"|"sensitive"|"tool_calls"|"user_stopped"|"error"|"unknown"

ClientMessage (done.message): { id, role:"USER"|"ASSISTANT"|"SYSTEM", content, reasoning?: string|null, reasoningParts?: string[]|null (only providers with part boundaries; absent = no steps, never infer), model?: string|null, feedback: "UP"|"DOWN"|null, createdAt: ISO string, conversationId?, versions?: [{id, model, createdAt}] (metadata only, oldest first), attachments: [{id, kind:"IMAGE"|"FILE", fileName, mimeType, size, url, width?, height?}], sources?, activity?, finishReason?, errorMessage?, promptTokens?, completionTokens?, costUsd? }. ClientQuota: { plan:"FREE"|"PRO"|"MAX"|"MAX20"|"OWNER", used, limit: number|null, remaining: number|null }

POST /api/chat pre-stream JSON errors: 401 {error:"Unauthorized"}; 429 {error:"You're sending messages too quickly. Please slow down."} (30 req/60s per user, owners exempt); 400 {error:"Invalid request."|"Message cannot be empty."|"Regenerate is not available in private chat."}; 403 {error:"policy_violation", message:"This request violates our Acceptable Use policy."}; 402 {error:"budget_exceeded", message:<human copy>} OR {error:"You've reached your monthly message limit. Upgrade your plan to keep chatting.", code:"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"}; 404 {error:"Conversation not found."|"Clarification card was not found."}; 409 {error:"Clarification card is no longer available."}; 503 no provider configured; 500 {error:"Couldn't start the chat: <detail>"}. Client rule: show data.message ?? data.error

POST /api/chat/cancel — body {generationId: string(8-120)} → 200 {ok: true, cancelled: boolean}. generationId is CLIENT-minted (web: crypto.randomUUID(), sent in the /api/chat body; server falls back to its own UUID if absent, which makes cancel impossible — always send one). Server-side: in-process Map on globalThis keyed by generationId, user-checked, 30-min GC; abort → generation catch persists partial (if any text/reasoning streamed) and emits done{finishReason:"user_stopped"}, else emits error{message:"Generation stopped before any output.", quota:<refunded>, finishReason:"user_stopped"}

Regenerate: POST /api/chat {conversationId, regenerate: true, model, canvasEnabled?, reasoningEffort?, connectors?, generationId} — NO message field. Server takes the trailing ASSISTANT row as stale, excludes it from the prompt, and only on success (or user-stop/network-error WITH partial output) atomically: (1) copies stale row ciphertext verbatim into MessageVersion, (2) deletes its artifacts, (3) OVERWRITES the Message row in place — same id, new content/reasoning/model/activity, feedback:=null, reasoningParts cleared-or-replaced, createdAt := now. done.message arrives under the ORIGINAL message id with versions[] grown. A failed regenerate leaves the old answer untouched. Private mode → 400

Edit-and-resend: PATCH /api/messages/[id] {content: string(1-50000)} → 200 {ok:true, version:{id, model, createdAt}}; only role USER, else 400 {error:"Only your messages can be edited."}. Server snapshots the pre-edit ciphertext to MessageVersion, updates content, deletes ALL later messages (their MessageVersions cascade) and later artifacts. Client then immediately POSTs /api/chat {conversationId, regenerate:true, ...} (no trailing assistant remains, so the server appends a fresh answer)

GET /api/messages/[id]/versions → {versions: [{id, content, reasoning?, model, promptTokens, completionTokens, sources?, createdAt}]} oldest-first, decrypted server-side; the live Message row is NOT included (it is always the newest version). Fetched lazily when the user pages back in the "‹ 2/3 ›" pager
POST /api/messages/[id]/feedback {feedback: "UP"|"DOWN"|null} → {ok:true}; ASSISTANT messages only (404 otherwise). Web client applies optimistically and fires-and-forgets

POST /api/chat/clarify — {message: string(≤50000), conversationId?: cuid|null, hasAttachments?: boolean, privateMode?: boolean} → ALWAYS 200 with PreflightClarificationResult: {needsClarification: boolean, reason: string, title: string, description: string, questions: [{id, question, type:"single-choice"|"multi-choice"|"text"|"text-long", options: string[], allowElse: boolean, elseLabel: string, elsePlaceholder: string, required: boolean}]}. Fails OPEN on every error/rate-limit/budget condition. Rate limit 60/60s. Web client wraps it in a 6s AbortController timeout and falls through to sending on any failure. On needsClarification the client shows a wizard and re-sends via /api/chat's preflightClarification field; the persisted visible user message becomes `<original>

Clarifications:
- <question>: <answer>` (formatPreflightClarificationVisibleMessage), while the model sees a transient one-generation prompt (formatPreflightClarificationModelMessage)

POST /api/chat/follow-ups {conversationId: cuid} → 200 {suggestions: string[]} (0-3 items, each 12-80 chars; empty array on any failure). 404 if conversation not owned; 429 at 30/60s. Pill text is sent VERBATIM as the next user message

POST /api/conversations/[id]/title (auto-title) — {phase?: "first_user"|"thinking"|"writing"|"completed"|"stopped", messages?: [{role:"USER"|"ASSISTANT", content ≤50000}](≤8)} → {title, titleSource: "default"|"ai"|"manual", renamed: boolean, projectId?: string|null, projectName?: string|null}. Rate limit 20/60s. Server only renames when titleSource allows (never over "manual"), sets titleSource:"ai", uses conditional updateMany to avoid clobbering concurrent renames; kicks a background project rename when the chat is in a project. NOTE: the SSE {type:"title"} chunk is parsed by both clients but the current chat route never emits it — this endpoint is the only auto-title path

POST /api/generate (image/video models) — {conversationId?: cuid, prompt: string(1-4000), model: string, edit?: {attachmentId, region?: {x,y,w,h each 0..1}, maskDataUrl?: "data:image/png;base64,..." ≤8MB decoded, transparent = area to edit}} → SSE with the same headers: meta {conversationId, userMessageId, title: <prompt slice 60 or "">} (no titleSource/generationId) → progress frames (video: queued→polling→downloading with last frame re-sent every 8s as keepalive; image: generating) → progress uploading → done {message (empty content + one attachment), artifacts: [], memoryUpdated: false, quota} | error {message, quota}. No generationId/cancel support. Errors pre-stream: 400/402/404/429 JSON. Rate limit 30/hr (owner 1000). New conversation title = prompt slice(0,60) fallback "New image"/"New video"

Private mode specifics: body {privateMode: true, message, privateHistory, model, webSearch?, reasoningEffort?, generationId?, client?, voiceMode?} — conversationId omitted. meta = {type:"meta", conversationId:"private", userMessageId: null, title:"Private chat", generationId}. done.message.id = `private-${Date.now()}`, artifacts always [], memoryUpdated always false; done still carries quota (private turns consume quota and record spend). Regenerate → 400; connectors never honored; deepResearch ignored; memory/canvas disabled in system prompt

Auth contract: getCurrentUser (src/lib/session.ts) checks the Authorization header FIRST — a Bearer native JWT (audience "juno-native", 10-min access TTL, refresh token 30d, PKCE S256 flow with redirect com.liammagnier.juno://auth/callback, see src/lib/native-auth-core.ts); an invalid bearer returns 401 with NO cookie fallback. Without the header it uses the next-auth session cookie (authjs.session-token) — the Swift app obtains this via CSRF → credentials POST and replays it from a Keychain-persisted URLSession cookie store

Swift request shape (BackendChatTransport.makeRequest — the app-side reference for a native client): persisted = {conversationId, message | regenerate:true, webSearch, canvasEnabled: true (always), model: <id>, client: "app", reasoningEffort?: <clamped rawValue>, generationId?}; private = {privateMode: true, message (" " if empty), privateHistory, webSearch, canvasEnabled: true, model, client: "app", reasoningEffort?, generationId?}. Headers: Content-Type: application/json, Accept: text/event-stream, timeoutInterval 300
behaviors: Optimistic send: append a pending USER message and an empty streaming ASSISTANT placeholder (temp ids `temp-${Date.now()}-${n}`) before the fetch; the meta frame swaps the pending user message's id to the server userMessageId (private mode: just clears pending)

Status machine (GenerationStatus): idle → checking (preflight clarify) → submitting (fetch sent) → thinking (first activity or reasoning frame; a "write" activity or first delta flips to writing; "reasoning" activity never demotes writing) → idle | error; stop() sets stopping. isBusy = checking|submitting|thinking|writing|stopping. Sends are refused while busy or while a clarification is pending

Frame handling: delta appends to content; reasoning folds through appendReasoningDelta (accumulate in a LOCAL variable outside the React updater — updaters run deferred; the part separator "\n\n" is inserted exactly at the provider-declared boundary; copy parts array before storing to avoid aliasing); sources REPLACE the whole array; activity events dedupe by event.id then append; ping is ignored; done replaces the placeholder wholesale with server message ({...chunk.message, finishReason: chunk.finishReason ?? message.finishReason, streaming:false}), merges artifacts by identifier, applies quota, fires onDone

Error frame: mark bubble error, finishReason := chunk.finishReason ?? "error"; if preservePartial && (content||reasoning) keep the streamed text and put chunk.message only in errorMessage, otherwise content := chunk.message too; toast it; apply quota if present

Stop: POST /api/chat/cancel {generationId}; only abort the local fetch if the response says cancelled:false (or the request fails) — a successful server cancel lets the stream deliver the partial done frame; ALSO arm a 5s fallback that force-aborts locally and stamps finishReason "user_stopped". On local abort keep whatever streamed and unmark streaming

Drop recovery (persisted chats only, after meta arrived): if the stream ends or throws without a done/error frame, mark the bubble "Connection interrupted — Juno keeps working in the background…", then poll GET /api/conversations/{id} (5s interval for 8 attempts, then 15s) up to RECOVERY_WINDOW_MS = 3,600,000+60,000 ms; the recovered answer is the first ASSISTANT message positioned after userMessageId whose id was never seen — with the regenerate wrinkle: locally-removed ids map id→createdAt, and a removed id counts as NEW only when its createdAt changed (the server overwrote the row in place). Abort recovery when a newer generation starts (sequence counter). On recover: swap into the placeholder, merge artifacts, fire onDone, toast success

Regenerate (client): pop ALL trailing ASSISTANT bubbles locally (recording id→createdAt in the removed-map), append a fresh streaming placeholder, POST with regenerate:true and NO message. The done frame arrives under the ORIGINAL message id. Edit-and-resend: PATCH the message, locally truncate to the edited message (appending the returned version metadata to its versions[] so the pager grows without refetch), then POST regenerate:true

Continue: literally send("Continue from where you left off.") as an ordinary message; the UI offers it for length/network_error finishes. Swift reuses the previous send's project/memory/webSearch context for it

Preflight clarify flow: before a normal chat send (not deep research, not media models, not empty text), POST /api/chat/clarify with a 6s timeout; on needsClarification hold the send (status idle, wizard shown), then re-send with preflightClarification {originalUserMessage, answers[{questionId, question, source, value}], skipped}; skip produces one answer {questionId:"skipped", question:"Pre-answer clarification", source:"skip", value:"Skipped clarification"}; any clarify failure falls through to sending. Deep-research sends bypass clarify entirely

Auto-title (client-driven): debounced POST /api/conversations/{id}/title, one request per phase per conversation, skipped when titleSource==="manual" or private mode; phases+delays: first_user 160ms (first non-empty user msg), thinking 240ms, writing 360ms (once streamed assistant content ≥24 chars), completed 420ms / stopped (after done; 80ms via onDone). Update the sidebar only if returned title/titleSource actually differ and current titleSource isn't manual; guard with a request sequence counter against out-of-order responses. Send up to 8 messages, content sliced to 4000 chars each

Follow-up pills: visible only when status idle, not private mode, last message is a non-empty non-error ASSISTANT; fetch POST /api/chat/follow-ups; pill click sends its text verbatim; a new send clears them

Private mode: client keeps full history locally and sends [...messages, newUserMsg] filtered to non-error USER/ASSISTANT as privateHistory on every send; conversationId/projectId omitted; canvasEnabled forced false; deepResearch/connectors suppressed; media models rejected with "Private chat only supports text chat right now."; nothing from the exchange is persisted server-side

Media models (modality image/video): route to /api/generate with {conversationId?, prompt, model} (+edit for region edits); the assistant placeholder shows progress {modality, stage} from progress frames; done delivers an empty-content message whose attachment is the generated file. Image edit is blocked in private mode
New-chat handoff: on meta with isNew (no prior conversationId), remember the id and upsert the sidebar conversation, but do NOT navigate mid-stream; after done, router.replace(`/chat/${id}`)

Heartbeats: server sends {type:"ping"} every 15s during chat generation (models can think silently for minutes); /api/generate re-sends the last progress frame every 8s during video jobs. Clients must tolerate long gaps otherwise

Server-side turn consumption: every generation consumes one quota message up front; failed generations (no useful output) refund it and return the corrected quota in the error frame; user_stopped does NOT refund. Mid-stream budget ceiling can abort a generation → treated exactly like user stop with the partial persisted, preceded by activity {kind:"warning", title:"Usage limit reached"}

Connectors: the connectors array persists onto conversation.activeConnectors server-side (omitting the field leaves the stored set untouched; sending an array replaces it), so toggles survive reloads; the web client seeds its toggle state from the conversation and sends the array on every message

Swift client differences: ignores activity and progress frames entirely (builds its own timeline); parses the (currently never-emitted) title frame into a meta event; maps done → .usage(promptTokens/completionTokens) + .cost(costUsd) + .persisted(message.id unless "private-" prefixed) + .finish(normalize(finishReason), unknown→stop); treats an error frame as a thrown error and lets the engine preserve partials; sends "[Image attached]" as the message when a user turn is image-only; caps inlined private-mode system context at 12,000 chars prefixed "[Context for this conversation — follow it silently, do not restate it]"

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/lib/reasoning-parts.ts — appendReasoningDelta/emptyReasoning/toStep/toSteps are pure functions shared by server and web client; port verbatim so the Windows app's reasoning panel is byte-identical to the website's
