Mirror its decisions in the Tauri client

/Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno/src/components/chat/follow-up-suggestions.tsx + the followUpsVisible memo in chat-view.tsx (~lines 338-342) — small, portable visibility rule + fetch for the follow-up pills
risks: Cancellation is best-effort and process-local: the generation registry is a Map on globalThis (src/lib/generation-cancel.ts), so POST /api/chat/cancel only works because the backend is a single `next start` Node process. Always mint and send generationId, and keep the web client's 5s local-abort fallback — but know a local abort alone lets the server finish, persist, and BILL the full reply

Closing the HTTP stream does NOT stop generation (route deliberately never passes req.signal to the model, and `after()` keeps the process working post-disconnect). A Windows client that kills the socket on window close without calling /api/chat/cancel will silently spend the user's budget; conversely this is what makes drop-recovery possible — implement both

Do not use EventSource: frames are POST-initiated and carry no `event:` field. Use streaming fetch. In Tauri 2, verify the chosen HTTP path (webview fetch vs plugin-http) actually exposes incremental ReadableStream reads — buffering the whole response breaks the product

Timing envelope: {type:"ping"} heartbeat every 15s, but generations can legally run ~1 hour (nginx proxy_read_timeout 3600s; RECOVERY_WINDOW_MS = 3,660,000ms is deliberately synced to it). Set client read timeouts accordingly (Swift uses 300s request timeout + recovery; the web client relies on the poll loop)

Regenerate mutates the assistant Message row IN PLACE: same id, new content, feedback wiped, createdAt bumped. Any client cache keyed by message id must accept content changes under a stable id, and drop-recovery must compare createdAt (the web client's locallyRemoved map exists precisely for this)

Two different 402 shapes ({error:"budget_exceeded", message} vs {error:..., code:"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"}) and a 403 {error:"policy_violation", message}; the human string lives in `message` when present, `error` otherwise — parse `data.message ?? data.error` everywhere (Swift does exactly this)

reasoningEffort can be sent freely from the full 6-tier enum — the server clamps per model (clampReasoningEffort) and never 400s on an unsupported tier (a past bug, per the route comment). Don't replicate per-model gating client-side beyond UX

The SSE `title` chunk and the body `clarification` field are live server contract but dormant in the web UI: title frames are never emitted (auto-title is the client-polled /api/conversations/[id]/title endpoint), and the in-chat clarification wizard component is orphaned. Parse both defensively (Swift does) but don't depend on receiving them

Attachments must be pre-uploaded (attachment rows with messageId null, owned by the user) before the chat POST links them via attachmentIds; the linking silently skips ids that don't match userId/messageId-null. The Swift app doesn't send attachmentIds at all — it substitutes "[Image attached]" text — so image-attachment parity with the website requires the upload flow, mapped separately

Native auth: Bearer access tokens live 10 minutes (NATIVE_ACCESS_TTL_SECONDS) — the Windows client needs proactive refresh; an expired/invalid bearer yields 401 with NO cookie fallback. A 1-hour generation outlives several access tokens, but only the initial request is authenticated — the stream itself needs no re-auth; the cancel call does

Send client:"app" (or agree on a new enum value — the zod schema only allows "web"|"app", anything else fails validation → 400 "Invalid request.") so spend-ledger attribution keeps working; same for the ≤5 connectors, ≤10 attachments, ≤24 privateHistory, ≤50000-char message caps — the server hard-rejects, not truncates

Private mode consumes quota and records spend despite persisting nothing; done.quota must still update the UI. Deep research is refused in private mode and voice mode, replaces native webSearch for its turn, and its costUsd on the done frame includes the planning spend — display, don't recompute
Rate limits to respect client-side: chat 30/min, clarify 60/min, follow-ups 30/min, title 20/min, generate 30/hour (owner email exempt on all)

Message content is encrypted at rest server-side (message-crypto) — irrelevant on the wire (SSE and JSON are plaintext), but MessageVersion snapshots copy ciphertext verbatim, which is why version rows always come from the dedicated /versions endpoint already decrypted; never try to read them elsewhere
Sources arrays are cumulative re-sends, not deltas — replace, never append, or you'll duplicate; activity events CAN be re-sent (client dedupes by event.id before appending)

summary: Juno's connectors subsystem has two backends surfaced as one directory: 6 native connectors (github, figma [oauth_app], notion [mcp_oauth w/ PKCE+DCR], apple-calendar, apple-mail, apple-music [credentials]) plus the Composio managed catalog (~1048 toolkits, ids "composio:<slug>"). All state lives server-side in the Prisma Connection table (AES-256-GCM encrypted tokens/credentials); linked connectors are exposed to models as remote MCP servers at generation time (native Anthropic mcp_servers, or an OpenAI-function-tool loop for other providers) — the client's only job is listing, connecting (browser OAuth redirect or in-app credentials POST), disconnecting, and sending up to 5 connector ids per chat message (persisted per-conversation as activeConnectors). OAuth connect/callback are cookie-bound browser navigations (302 chains ending at /connections?connected=X or ?error=code); the existing Swift app doesn't run OAuth natively — it opens the website in the default browser and re-fetches GET /api/connectors when its view appears. Scheduled tasks are a simple authed CRUD (/api/tasks) over a structured schedule (cadence DAILY|WEEKDAYS|WEEKLY|MONTHLY + hour/minute/weekday/monthday + IANA timezone, default Europe/Paris); execution happens in a separate pm2 worker (60s tick, atomic claim via nextRunAt bump), results land in a normal chat conversation (task.conversationId), and each task carries its latest run summary (status running|done|error|budget, error text, costMicroUsd). Plan gates: FREE=0, PRO=3, MAX+=10 tasks. contracts: AUTH (all endpoints below): NextAuth session cookie OR native bearer — getCurrentUser (src/lib/session.ts) checks the Authorization header first; 'Authorization: Bearer <native token>' is authoritative and never falls back to cookies. Unauthed JSON routes → 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}; unauthed redirect routes → 302 /sign-in. Error envelope everywhere: {error: string, message?: string} (Swift decodes both fields).

GET /api/connectors → 200 {connectors: Connector[], composioConfigured: boolean}. Connector = {id, kind, label, description, capability, configured: boolean, connected: boolean, accountLabel: string|null, connectedAt: string|null (ISO)}. kind ∈ "oauth_app"|"mcp_oauth"|"credentials"|"composio_app". Native ids: "github","figma","notion","apple-calendar","apple-mail","apple-music" (always listed, configured=false when server lacks env keys). Composio entries appear ONLY when connected: id="composio:<slug>", kind="composio_app", configured=true, connected=true, description=`Use ${label} through Juno.`, capability=`Let the model use your connected ${label} account.` (src/app/api/connectors/route.ts).
DELETE /api/connectors/[id] → 200 {ok:true}; 404 {error:"Unknown connector."} for non-native ids. Deletes the user's Connection row (native connectors only — Composio has its own DELETE).

GET /api/connectors/[id]/connect → pure 302 browser navigation (NEVER call via fetch): requires signed-in session in that browser; sets httpOnly cookies oauth_nonce_<id> (maxAge 600, sameSite lax) and, for notion (mcp_oauth), oauth_session_<id> (encrypted PKCE verifier + DCR client); redirects to provider consent. Failure redirects: /connections?error=unknown|use_credentials|not_configured. State = HMAC-signed JSON {u:userId, c:connectorId, n:nonce}.

GET /api/connectors/[id]/callback?code&state (+provider error params) → 302 to /connections?connected=<id> on success, or /connections?error=denied|bad_state|exchange_failed|not_configured. Stores tokens encrypted on Connection (upsert on userId+provider). expiresAt = now+expires_in, else now+3600s if a refresh token exists, else null (GitHub: never expires).

POST /api/connectors/[id]/credentials (credentials kind only; Content-Type: application/json). apple-calendar / apple-mail body: {appleId: string, appPassword: string} — validated live (CalDAV principal lookup caldav.icloud.com / IMAP login imap.mail.me.com:993). apple-music body: {musicUserToken: string} — validated via storefront lookup. 200 {ok:true, accountLabel} (accountLabel = appleId, or "Apple Music · US"). 401 {error:"invalid_credentials", message} (message includes app-specific-password hint); 400 {error:"bad_request"|"not_configured", message}; 502 {error:"validation_failed", message}; 404 {error:"Unknown connector."}.

GET /api/connectors/apple-music/dev-token → 200 {token: string} (short-lived MusicKit developer JWT for MusicKit JS authorize()); 400 {error:"not_configured", message}; 500 {error:"token_failed", message}.

GET /api/connectors/composio/catalog?q=<≤100 chars>&cursor=<≤300>&connected=1&category=<curated id> → 200 {items: ComposioAppItem[], cursor?: string, totalPages: number, total?: number, categories: {id, label, count?: number}[]}. ComposioAppItem = {id: "composio:<slug>", slug, name, logo: string|null, connected: boolean, connecting: boolean, noAuth: boolean, managedAuth: boolean, status: string|null, connectedAt: string|null}. managedAuth=false ⇒ Connect will fail until user adds own OAuth app in Composio dashboard. 429 {error} with Retry-After header (seconds); 503 {error:"Composio is not configured"}; 502 {error:"Could not load the Composio catalog"}. Curated category ids: productivity, developer-tools, artificial-intelligence, team-collaboration, email, calendar, documents, project-management, crm, marketing-automation, analytics, ecommerce, accounting, images-&-design, customer-support, file-management-&-storage, video-&-audio, news-&-lifestyle. Page size default 30 (clamped 1–50); cursor encodes page+size, keep limit constant across a paged run.

GET /api/connectors/composio/[slug]/connect → 302 browser navigation: either provider consent URL, or straight back /connections?connected=<slug> (no-auth toolkit or already-active account), or /connections?error=not_configured|unknown|rate_limited|connection_busy|needs_auth_config&app=<slug>|exchange_failed. Slug regex gate: ^[a-z0-9_][a-z0-9_-]{1,80}$.

GET /api/connectors/composio/[slug]/callback?flow=<uuid36>&status=<success|…> → 302 /connections?connected=<slug> or ?error=bad_state|denied|rate_limited|connection_busy|exchange_failed. Server waits up to 15s (waitForConnection) for the account to go ACTIVE.

DELETE /api/connectors/composio/[slug] → 200 {ok:true}; 409 {error:"Connection operation in progress"}; 429 {error} + Retry-After; 502 {error:"Could not disconnect app"}; 404 {error:"Unknown app"}. Also revokes the credential at Composio (revoke_on_delete).

CHAT INTEGRATION: POST /api/chat body field `connectors: string[]` (zod max 5) — connector ids incl. "composio:<slug>". undefined = leave the conversation's stored set untouched; [] = clear it. Server persists de-duped set on Conversation.activeConnectors (String[] in prisma), returned to the client when opening /chat/[id] (initialConnectors). Ignored when privateMode=true. SSE: connectors surface via the existing `activity` event: {type:"activity", event:{id, createdAt, kind:"tool", title:"Connected tools ready", detail:"GitHub · Notion"}} once at start, and {kind:"tool", title:"Using <connector label>", detail:"<tool_name>"} per tool call (non-Anthropic tool loop). No dedicated SSE event type for connectors.

MCP (served BY Juno, called by model infra, not the desktop client): POST /api/mcp/[connector] for apple-calendar|apple-mail|apple-music — stateless JSON-RPC 2.0 over POST (JSON response mode; GET/DELETE → 405). Auth: Authorization: Bearer <connector-token> where token = base64url(JSON {userId, connectorId, exp(unix ms)}) + "." + HMAC-SHA256(base64url payload, key "juno:connector-token:"+AUTH_SECRET) base64url; TTL 15 min. Methods: initialize (protocolVersion negotiation among 2024-11-05|2025-03-26|2025-06-18), ping, tools/list, tools/call. Tools — apple-calendar: list_calendars, list_events, create_event, delete_event; apple-mail: list_mailboxes, search_messages, read_message, unread_count; apple-music: search_catalog, list_playlists, recently_played, add_to_playlist. /api/mcp/composio/[slug] (GET/POST/DELETE): bearer-authed proxy (connectorId must equal "composio:<slug>") forwarding request headers accept, content-type, mcp-protocol-version, mcp-session-id, last-event-id and response headers content-type, cache-control, mcp-session-id, retry-after to the Composio session MCP URL.

GET /api/tasks → 200 {tasks: Task[], limit: number}. Task (serializeTask, src/lib/scheduled-tasks.ts) = {id, name, prompt, model, modelName, cadence: "DAILY"|"WEEKDAYS"|"WEEKLY"|"MONTHLY", hour: 0-23, minute: 0-59, weekday: number|null (0=Sun…6=Sat), monthday: number|null (1-28), timezone: string (IANA), webSearch: boolean, enabled: boolean, lastRunAt: string|null, nextRunAt: string (ISO), conversationId: string|null, createdAt: string, latestRun: {id, status: "running"|"done"|"error"|"budget", error: string|null, costMicroUsd: number, startedAt: string, finishedAt: string|null} | null}. limit by plan: FREE 0, PRO 3, MAX/OWNER 10.

POST /api/tasks body {name: 1-80 trimmed, prompt: 1-4000, model: string (must resolve to a chat, non-comingSoon model the plan allows), cadence, hour, minute, weekday?: 0-6 (required iff WEEKLY), monthday?: 1-28 (required iff MONTHLY), timezone?: string (default "Europe/Paris", validated via Intl), webSearch?: boolean (default true)} → 201 {task}. Errors: 400 {error:"Invalid input"}; 403 {error:"plan_locked", message:"Scheduled tasks are part of Pro…"}; 403 {error:"task_limit", message:"Your plan allows N scheduled task(s)."}; 400 {error:"Pick a chat model for this task."}; 403 {error:"Your plan doesn't include this model."}; 400 {error:"Unknown timezone — use an IANA name like Europe/Paris."}.

PATCH /api/tasks/[id] — any subset of the POST fields plus enabled: boolean; weekday/monthday accept null. Server validates the MERGED schedule (cadence flip must ship its day field: 400 "Weekly tasks need a weekday." / "Monthly tasks need a day of the month.") and always recomputes nextRunAt (re-enabling restarts from now). → 200 {task}; 404 {error:"Not found"}.
DELETE /api/tasks/[id] → 200 {ok:true}; 404 {error:"Not found"}. Runs cascade-delete; the results conversation is kept (a normal chat, linked via /chat/{conversationId}).

risks: OAuth connect flows (native oauth_app/mcp_oauth AND Composio) are strictly cookie-bound browser navigations: /connect requires a signed-in session in the browser that opens it, sets httpOnly state/nonce cookies, and /callback consumes them in that same browser, ending in a redirect to the WEBSITE's /connections page. A Tauri client cannot drive this with bearer-token fetches. Options: open the system browser at <appUrl> (user needs a web session there) like the Mac app does, or embed a webview sharing the site session. Completion detection = re-poll GET /api/connectors (or catalog with connected=1) — there is no push/webhook/deep-link back to the native app today.

Credentials connectors ARE fully linkable from a native client: POST /api/connectors/[id]/credentials and GET /api/connectors/apple-music/dev-token accept the native Authorization: Bearer token (getCurrentUser checks the header first). Apple Music still needs MusicKit JS (Apple CDN script + Apple sign-in popup) — do it in the Tauri WebView, not in Rust.

`connectors` field semantics in POST /api/chat: undefined ≠ []. Omitting leaves the conversation's persisted activeConnectors untouched; sending [] clears them. Sending >5 ids fails zod and 400s the whole chat request — dedupe and cap client-side. Composio ids must be the full "composio:<slug>" form.

302-redirect endpoints (connect/callback, composio connect/callback) must never be fetch()'d from the app: an HTTP client will follow the redirect into provider HTML or the sign-in page. Treat them exclusively as browser URLs.

Composio catalog is rate-limited per user per action bucket (catalog/connect/disconnect/callback) → handle 429 + Retry-After (seconds, string header). Catalog cursor encodes page AND page size — keep limit constant across a paged run or pages misalign. Composio 'connected' on a tile = local ACTIVE row AND live remote account (cross-check can lag or fall back to local on Composio outage); 'connecting' has a 60s UI grace window; concurrent connection changes 409 ("connection_busy").

needs_auth_config error (Composio hosts no shared OAuth app, e.g. twitter) is NOT retryable — replicate the web's 'Set up in Composio' affordance (link to https://platform.composio.dev/marketplace/<slug>) instead of a Connect button when managedAuth=false.

The 'In chats' toggle (localStorage "juno:mcp:enabled") is page-local and never consulted by the composer or server — don't assume it gates anything. Real gating is per-conversation via the chat request.

Task schedule gotchas: weekday is 0=Sun…6=Sat (web UI just displays Mon-first); monthday hard-capped 1–28; server default timezone is "Europe/Paris" (NOT the user's) — always send the user's IANA zone explicitly from the Windows client. PATCH validates the merged schedule: changing cadence to WEEKLY/MONTHLY without weekday/monthday 400s. nextRunAt is server-computed; toggling enabled=true recomputes it from now. latestRun.status is a plain string with 4 values incl. "budget" (budget-skipped run); costMicroUsd is integer micro-USD (divide by 1_000_000 for $).

Tasks are plan-gated: expect 403 {error:"plan_locked"} (FREE) and {error:"task_limit"} — map both to distinct UI (upsell vs. cap message). GET /api/tasks returns limit so the client can pre-disable creation. Task runs fire from a separate pm2 worker ("juno-scheduler", 60s tick) — if that worker isn't running on a deployment, tasks simply never execute; the API gives no health signal.

Connector env prerequisites decide `configured`: GitHub/Figma need *_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/SECRET, Apple Music needs APPLE_MUSIC_TEAM_ID/KEY_ID/PRIVATE_KEY, Notion only needs its default NOTION_MCP_URL, apple-calendar/mail always configured, Composio needs COMPOSIO_API_KEY (composioConfigured flag in GET /api/connectors). summary: Juno's billing/usage/account plane is a set of cookie-authed JSON routes under /api/*. Billing is Stripe-hosted: POST /api/stripe/checkout returns a Checkout URL for plan PRO|MAX|MAX20, POST /api/stripe/portal returns a Billing Portal URL, and a signature-verified webhook syncs the Subscription row (plan flips are async). Plans (src/lib/plans.ts): FREE ($0, browse-only, monthlyMessages=0), PRO ($20), MAX "Max x5" ($100), MAX20 "Max x20" ($200), OWNER (env-granted). Paid plans have unlimited message counts but are gated by a per-plan monthly EUR budget (PRO 11€, MAX 55€, MAX20 110€) tracked in integer micro-USD in an ApiSpend ledger (src/lib/spend.ts); the budget gate returns HTTP 402 and a mid-stream ceiling halts generation. GET /api/profile/usage is the canonical usage snapshot (quota + spend + rolling 5-hour/weekly windows), GET /api/profile/stats a token heatmap, and POST /api/agent/usage the start/record/refund accounting loop for Code turns. Settings are PATCH-only (/api/settings, 12 optional keys); reads come from bootstrap (web SSR AppBootstrap or Bearer-authed GET /api/v1/bootstrap which returns the raw Prisma row). Search is title-only (messages are encrypted at rest): server GET /api/conversations?q= does insensitive title-contains; the web UI actually filters its in-memory list (sidebar + Cmd-K palette). Library GET /api/library?kind=IMAGE|FILE has no text query. i18n is machine-translated on demand via GET /api/i18n/translations?locale&ids= against a generated hash-id catalog. Announcements: GET /api/announcements returns the one live, undismissed announcement; POST /api/announcements/{id}/dismiss persists dismissal; owner-only /api/admin/announcements CRUD exists.
contracts: POST /api/stripe/checkout (src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts) — cookie auth. Body: {"plan": "PRO"|"MAX"|"MAX20"} (zod enum). 200 {"url": <Stripe Checkout session URL>}. Errors: 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}, 400 {"error":"Invalid plan."}, 503 {"error":"Billing is not configured."} or {"error":"Plan price is not configured."}. Server sets success_url `${appUrl}/chat?upgraded=1`, cancel_url `${appUrl}/upgrade`, allow_promotion_codes, metadata {userId, plan}.

POST /api/stripe/portal (src/app/api/stripe/portal/route.ts) — cookie auth, no body. 200 {"url": <Billing Portal URL>} with return_url `${appUrl}/settings`. 400 {"error":"No billing account found."} if no stripeCustomerId; 503 if Stripe unconfigured.

POST /api/stripe/webhook — server-only (header `stripe-signature`); handles checkout.session.completed + customer.subscription.created/updated/deleted; maps Stripe status → SubStatus enum ACTIVE|TRIALING|PAST_DUE|CANCELED|INCOMPLETE and updates Subscription {plan,status,stripeSubscriptionId,stripePriceId,currentPeriodEnd,cancelAtPeriodEnd}. Price ids from env STRIPE_PRICE_PRO/STRIPE_PRICE_MAX/STRIPE_PRICE_MAX20 (src/lib/stripe.ts). Not called by clients; plan changes land asynchronously after checkout.

GET /api/profile/usage (src/app/api/profile/usage/route.ts) — cookie auth. 200 {"quota":{"plan":"FREE"|"PRO"|"MAX"|"MAX20"|"OWNER","used":int,"limit":int|null,"remaining":int|null},"spend":{"spentMicroUsd":int,"budgetMicroUsd":int|null,"remainingMicroUsd":int|null,"eurPerUsd":number,"windows":{"session":{"spentMicroUsd","budgetMicroUsd","pct","resetsAtMs"},"weekly":{same}},"billing":{"renewsAtMs":epochMs|null,"cancelAtPeriodEnd":bool}}}. budgetMicroUsd null=unlimited (OWNER), 0=blocked (FREE). session = rolling 5-hour window, weekly = rolling 7-day, both anchored to subscription createdAt/currentPeriodEnd; pct = spend/windowBudget (0..∞, 1 = on pace for full period budget). Swift mirror: juno-app/Juno/Services/Backend/SyncModels.swift QuotaDTO/UsageWindowDTO/SpendDTO/ProfileUsageDTO.

GET /api/profile/stats (src/app/api/profile/stats/route.ts) — cookie auth. 200 {"daily":{"YYYY-MM-DD":{"tokens":int,"count":int}},"models":[{"model":string,"count":int,"tokens":int}] sorted by count desc,"totalTokens":int,"totalMessages":int,"memberSince":iso|null}. Covers last 371 days (53 weeks), max 50k message rows; totalMessages counts ASSISTANT role only.

POST /api/agent/usage (src/app/api/agent/usage/route.ts) — cookie auth, Code-mode accounting. {"phase":"start"} → checks € budget then consumes one message: 200 {"ok":true,"quota":QuotaStatus} or 402 {"error":<msg>,"code":"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"} (budget) / 402 {"error":"You've reached your monthly usage limit...","code":"QUOTA_EXCEEDED","quota":...} (message cap, FREE only). {"phase":"record","model":"provider:id","promptTokens":n,"completionTokens":n} → 200 {"ok":true}; writes Usage aggregate + ApiSpend row (kind "code", source "app"). {"phase":"refund"} → 200 {"ok":true,"quota":...}. Unknown phase → 400 {"error":"Unknown phase."}.

Chat-route enforcement (src/app/api/chat/route.ts): per-user rate limit 30 req/60s → 429 {"error":"You're sending messages too quickly. Please slow down."} (NO Retry-After or X-RateLimit-* headers anywhere — bodies only; limiter is Postgres-backed, src/lib/rate-limit.ts). Pre-stream budget gate → 402 {"error":"budget_exceeded","message":<human sentence from budgetExceededMessage()>}. Message-cap gate → 402 {"error":"You've reached your monthly message limit. Upgrade your plan to keep chatting.","code":"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"}. NOTE two distinct 402 shapes: budget uses error="budget_exceeded"+message; quota uses error=<human>+code="QUOTA_EXCEEDED". Mid-stream: running cost (tokens × per-token micro-USD rates) hitting remainingMicroUsd halts generation, emits SSE activity event {kind:"warning",title:"Usage limit reached",detail:"Stopped to stay within your plan's budget."}, saves+bills the partial. SSE done frame carries server-authoritative costUsd and quota:ClientQuota; error frame may carry quota (refunded).

PATCH /api/settings (src/app/api/settings/route.ts) — cookie auth, PATCH-only (NO GET). Body keys all optional: theme "light"|"dark"|"system"; accent (max 30, regex ^([a-z]+|#[0-9a-fA-F]{6})$; built-in ids coral|teal|violet|amber|sage); defaultModel (must pass isModelId → 400 {"error":"Unknown model"}); personality ∈ ["default","concise","encouraging","socratic","formal","nerdy"]; customInstructions ≤4000; responseLanguage ≤40 (English language NAME like "French", or "auto"); uiLocale ≤35 ("auto" or BCP-47, canonicalized e.g. fr_fr→fr-FR, else 400 {"error":"Unknown locale"}); memoryEnabled bool; voiceId string≤100|null; favoriteModels string[](each ≤120, max 200); emailBudgetAlerts bool; emailWeeklyDigest bool. 200 {"ok":true}; 400 {"error":"Invalid input"}.

Settings document (Prisma model Settings, prisma/schema.prisma:256): {id, userId, theme THEME enum LIGHT|DARK|SYSTEM (default SYSTEM), accent (default "coral"), defaultModel (default "claude-sonnet-4-6"), customInstructions "", responseLanguage "auto", uiLocale "auto", personality "default", memoryEnabled true, voiceId null, favoriteModels [], emailBudgetAlerts true, emailWeeklyDigest false, createdAt, updatedAt}. Web ClientSettings (src/lib/app-data.ts) lowercases theme and drops id/userId/timestamps; v1 bootstrap returns the RAW row (uppercase theme).

Web SSR bootstrap AppBootstrap (src/lib/app-data.ts, not an HTTP route — inlined into the page): {user:{id,name,email,image}, settings:ClientSettings, quota:{plan,used,limit,remaining}, spend:{spentMicroUsd,budgetMicroUsd,eurPerUsd,windows:{session:{pct,resetsAtMs},weekly:{pct,resetsAtMs}},billing:{renewsAtMs,cancelAtPeriodEnd}}, conversations:ClientConversation[], folders:[{id,name}], features:{billing,serverStt,serverTts,ttsProvider "openai"|"elevenlabs"|null,storage,webSearch,deepResearch,email,providers:string[],isOwner}}. features.billing=isStripeConfigured() — gates all upgrade UI.

Max 30 ids/request; ids must match /^[a-f0-9]{16}$/ and exist in generated UI_TRANSLATION_CATALOG (src/lib/i18n-catalog.generated.ts: [{id,source}] — English source strings keyed by hash). 200 {"translations":{"<id>":"<translated>"}} with Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, s-maxage=2592000, stale-while-revalidate=86400. locale invalid → 400 {translations:{}}. Agent/Code usage protocol rules (BACKEND_API.md §8b, port of core/src/usage.ts): reserve with phase:start ONCE per user turn before any model call (not per loop iteration); ONLY HTTP 402 blocks the turn — any other failure (transient 401/5xx, bad JSON, network) fails OPEN; at turn end phase:record cumulative tokens, or phase:refund if the turn produced no billable output; never report BYOK (user's own key) turns.

Search UX: the web sidebar and Cmd-K command palette filter the ALREADY-LOADED bootstrap conversations array client-side by lowercase title substring (src/components/app/app-sidebar.tsx, command-palette.tsx) — no network per keystroke; palette shows max 6 conversation hits when a query is typed (4 when empty) plus static commands grouped Actions/Conversations/Navigate/Appearance. Keyboard: Cmd/Ctrl+K toggle palette, Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O new chat, Cmd/Ctrl+/ shortcuts dialog. Server-side ?q= exists for clients that don't hold the full list.

Announcement popup: fetched once per route change (skipped on /admin routes); double dismissal guard — localStorage key "juno:dismissed_announcements" (JSON array of ids) checked before showing, AND server POST dismiss; dismiss is optimistic (update local state + localStorage first, fire-and-forget the POST). Closing via X, "Not now", or following any link all dismiss. hrefs starting with "/" navigate in-app, otherwise open externally. videoUrl autoplays muted+looped with imageUrl as poster; falls back image → provider logo → sparkles icon. Popup defers until first-run onboarding finishes.

Settings writes are optimistic + fire-and-forget PATCH (e.g. theme toggle in command palette updates UI immediately, then PATCHes {theme} and swallows errors). The Swift app mirrors ONLY customInstructions+responseLanguage up (debounced 1.5s, clamped to 4000/40 chars so the PATCH can't 400) because the server builds persisted-chat system prompts from prisma.settings — a Windows client must do the same or server-side chats ignore local personalization.

Error handling rules (BACKEND_API.md §9): any 401 {"error":"Unauthorized"} → re-authenticate then retry once; standard error body {"error":<human>} with optional machine "code"; handle 400/401/402/404/409/429/5xx. Both 402 shapes must be handled: {error:"budget_exceeded",message} (chat) and {error,code:"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"[,quota]} (quota/agent).

Quota lifecycle during chat: consumeMessage reserves atomically pre-stream; failed generations refund (SSE error frame may carry the refunded quota); SSE done frame carries fresh quota + server-authoritative cache-aware costUsd — prefer it over client-side estimates (thread reloads re-derive an upper-bound from stored tokens).

risks: No GET /api/settings exists — settings reads come only from bootstrap (web SSR AppBootstrap, or Bearer-authed GET /api/v1/bootstrap). The v1 bootstrap returns the RAW Prisma row (theme UPPERCASE "SYSTEM", includes id/userId/timestamps) while the web PATCH/read contract uses lowercase theme strings — normalize in the client.

Two auth schemes coexist: /api/* account/billing/usage/search/announcement routes are session-cookie-authed (NextAuth cookie the Swift app replays via URLSession); /api/v1/* uses Authorization: Bearer with the native-auth token flow. A Tauri client must hold the cookie jar for the legacy routes even if it adopts v1.

Two different 402 payload shapes: chat budget gate → {"error":"budget_exceeded","message":...}; message-quota and agent/usage gates → {"error":<human>,"code":"QUOTA_EXCEEDED"}. Handling only one silently breaks the other path.

429 responses carry NO Retry-After or X-RateLimit headers — only {"error":...} bodies. Known windows: chat 30/60s per user, export 5/hr, account-delete 3/hr, avatar 20/hr, i18n 200/hr/IP + 4000/hr global. Clients must self-impose backoff (web i18n uses 5 min after 429).

Money is integer micro-USD end-to-end, but budgets are DEFINED in EUR — display in € by multiplying micro-USD by eurPerUsd from the payload. v1 bootstrap serializes BigInt token counters as strings ("promptTokens":"12345").

Search is title-only: message bodies are encrypted at rest (src/lib/message-crypto.ts) so SQL contains cannot see them. Do not build or promise full-text message search against this backend. The `archived` filter option in listConversations is NOT exposed via query param on /api/conversations.

Stripe checkout/portal URLs redirect back to WEBSITE routes (/chat?upgraded=1, /upgrade, /settings) — no custom-scheme deep link exists, so a native client opening them in the browser strands the user on the website; plan changes only land after the webhook fires (refresh /api/profile/usage on window refocus).

Plan entitlement requires Subscription.status ACTIVE or TRIALING; PAST_DUE/CANCELED/INCOMPLETE degrade to FREE immediately (src/lib/usage.ts getUserPlan). FREE has monthlyMessages=0 and budget €0 — it is browse-only; every model is floored at PRO (effectiveMinPlan). OWNER is granted via OWNER_EMAILS env, not purchasable.

Enforcement split: the message counter only actually blocks FREE (paid plans have limit=null); the € budget (checkBudget, HTTP 402) is the real paid-plan gate, plus a mid-stream cost ceiling that halts generation and still bills the partial. The 5-hour/weekly windows are DISPLAY ONLY — never enforce them client-side.
features.billing in bootstrap (isStripeConfigured()) gates upgrade UI — hide checkout affordances when false; checkout/portal return 503 on unconfigured deployments.

i18n catalog ids are 16-hex hashes generated by juno/scripts/generate-i18n-catalog.mjs from the website's source strings — a Tauri client can only use /api/i18n/translations if it ships/regenerates the same catalog; the route is unauthenticated but aggressively rate-limited and translations are LLM-generated (expect partial results and 503s).
GET /api/v1/bootstrap announcements is a hard-coded [] stub — real announcements only exist on cookie-authed GET /api/announcements (which returns null when signed out rather than 401).

GET /api/announcements returns at most ONE announcement (newest live undismissed); the web adds a localStorage dismissal guard because the server dismiss POST is fire-and-forget — replicate both layers or users may see re-shows offline.

DELETE /api/account (no confirmation) and POST /api/account/delete (email confirmation) both exist and both hard-delete; wire native UI to the confirming variant. DELETE /api/conversations deletes ALL conversations at once — dangerous, confirm in UI.

SSE activity event payload (BACKEND_API.md §157): activity → event = { id, kind, title, detail?, url?, createdAt }, kind ∈ context|model|reasoning|search|visit|write|usage|done|warning|tool. Internal LlmEvent stream vocabulary ported 1:1 from the web (MIGRATION.md): text / reasoning / sources / tool / usage / finish.

FinishReason canonical strings (Juno/Models/CoreTypes.swift): stop, length, tool_calls, sensitive, model_context_window_exceeded, network_error, user_stopped, error, unknown. normalize() maps raw provider strings: end_turn|complete|finished→stop; max_tokens|max_output_tokens→length; tool_use→tool_calls; content_filter|safety→sensitive; context_length_exceeded→model_context_window_exceeded; network→network_error; cancelled|canceled|abort→user_stopped. allowsContinue == (length || network_error).

MessageRole raw values 'USER' / 'ASSISTANT' / 'SYSTEM'; message feedback stored as literal strings 'UP' / 'DOWN' (nullable); conversation titleSource value 'manual' guards against auto-title overwrite; conversation kindRaw 'chat' vs code kind 'code'.

GenerationStatus client state machine: idle → checking → submitting → thinking → writing (+ stopping, error); isBusy = checking|submitting|thinking|writing|stopping. UI strings: 'Checking', 'Thinking', 'Writing'.

ReasoningEffort raw values low|medium|high|max; Anthropic extended-thinking budget tokens 1024/4096/8000/12000; model reasoning descriptor fields used by UI: supportsReasoning, reasoning.canDisable, reasoning.onOff, reasoning.tiers; effort→output-price multiplier table (ModelSelectorDetailPane): nil=1.0, low=1.3, medium=1.8, high=2.4, max=3.0.

docs/BACKEND_API.md + docs/CONTRACTS.md — copy into the Windows repo as the wire-contract reference; RUNNING.md documents the local backend workflow (npm run dev in the Next.js repo at localhost:3000) that the Windows app will develop against.
risks: Two Code modes coexist: RootView gates on @AppStorage('junoClassicCode') — classic CodeModeView (3-zone cockpit) vs the newer EngineWorkbenchView (default, engine-driven). Both share the inspector/approval components. Windows should target ONLY the engine workbench; the classic cockpit exists solely because computer-use hasn't reached engine parity (REBUILD/RootView comments).

Docs disagree with code in places — MIGRATION.md describes the standalone/direct-provider v1 architecture and REBUILD.md a sidecar architecture, but the SHIPPING app is a thin client of the Juno backend (DESIGN.md v2 + RUNNING.md): server holds provider keys, Keychain holds only the session token, BYOK is an Advanced escape hatch. Build the Windows app as a backend client; treat BACKEND_API.md as the source of truth, not MIGRATION.md's 'direct provider calls'.

Apple-only subsystems needing Windows replacements or explicit exclusion: Computer Use (ScreenCaptureKit + Accessibility — HUD/consent/inspector tab all mac-gated), SFSpeechRecognizer dictation + AVSpeechSynthesizer TTS (on-device voice overlay & read-aloud), security-scoped bookmarks for workspace folders (Tauri needs its own persisted-folder-access story), Keychain (→ Windows Credential Manager via Tauri keyring), NSSavePanel/NSOpenPanel, 'Reveal in Finder'/'Open in Terminal' (→ Explorer / Windows Terminal), Liquid Glass materials (re-express with CSS translucency; Windows 11 Mica/Acrylic optional).

Google sign-in cannot be done with in-app credential POST — the app uses a browser-based 'Continue with Juno web' flow (WebAuthService with a callback). Windows needs a loopback-server or deep-link (tauri-plugin-deep-link) equivalent; BACKEND_API.md notes the Google-OAuth-for-native limitation.

Auth is cookie-based (authjs session cookie, name varies by scheme: authjs.session-token vs __Secure-authjs.session-token). The Rust/JS HTTP client must implement the CSRF-token + cookie dance and persist/reattach the cookie across restarts; naive fetch without cookie jar will 401 (the /api/models sanity check 401s until signed in).

SSE parsing: frames are separated by \n\n with 'data:' prefixes and NO 'event:' field — the discriminator is the JSON 'type'. Response relies on X-Accel-Buffering: no; make sure the Windows HTTP stack doesn't buffer (use streaming fetch/reqwest, not a body-collecting client).

ID discipline is load-bearing: the client claims a server cuid via POST /api/conversations BEFORE the first /api/chat send so threads sync from message one; offline/BYOK falls back to a local UUID and the code distinguishes them with id.contains('-'). Replicate exactly, including the 'never eagerly create on New-chat tap' rule (avoids empty synced rows) and the double-send guard while creation is in flight.

Private mode guarantees are absolute in the native app: separate in-memory store, no attachments persisted, no memory injection/extraction, server told privateMode, transcript destroyed on exit. Any Windows shortcut (e.g. reusing the main store with a flag) risks leaking incognito data into sync/memory.

Artifact HTML preview injects https://cdn.tailwindcss.com into wrapped fragments — the sandbox therefore needs network for that CDN (JS enabled for HTML/React types). Decide consciously for Windows: same CDN dependency, or vendor Tailwind inline; and mirror the strict navigation policy or untrusted model HTML can open URLs/popups.
Reduce-motion is threaded through every animation; plan a prefers-reduced-motion pass from day one rather than retrofitting.

Model catalog is server-synced but the app ships an offline ModelCatalog fallback whose display order must match /api/models (lab order → intelligence desc → name); 'comingSoon' models must be unselectable everywhere including the thinking-slider commit path (ModelSelectorView.setEffort replicates the guard).

Usage meters tick locally every 30s WITHOUT refetching and refresh on appear/foreground + after 402s; budgets are micro-USD converted to EUR via spend.eurPerUsd — currency display falls back to $ when the rate is missing.

Update pipeline (UpdateService: background checks, staged download, 'Relaunch to update' chip) is a custom macOS implementation — Windows should use Tauri updater but keep the quiet-chip UX (never a modal popup).

The voice relay is optional infrastructure: every voice entry point checks BackendConfiguration.voiceRelayURL != nil and falls back to the on-device loop. If Windows ships without local STT/TTS, hide the voice orb entirely when no relay is configured rather than showing dead UI.

Streaming-state guards to preserve: sidebar Delete suppressed for the streaming conversation; private-mode toggle blocked while generating; session delete blocked while its engine is running; composer send disabled while busy; engine survives mode/session switches (store-owned) — losing any of these on Windows causes data-loss bugs the Swift comments explicitly fixed.
summary: Windows parity checklist for the Juno native app (SwiftUI, /Users/liammagnier/Desktop/workspace/juno-app), mapped from docs (DESIGN/REBUILD/MIGRATION/RUNNING.md) and direct reads of all Features/** view+viewmodel files. The shipping app is a thin client of the Next.js backend (localhost:3000 / Vercel): authjs cookie session, server-held provider keys, SSE chat streaming, upsert-only sync of chats/projects/memory/connectors/models/usage. Its UX spine: a 3-mode shell (Chat ⌘1 / Cowork ⌘2 / Code ⌘3) with a collapsible sidebar (date-grouped recents, pin/rename/delete, Library/Artifacts/Connections/Projects sheets, account footer), ⌘K command palette, 3-step onboarding (sign-in default, BYOK hidden in Advanced), a rich chat surface (serif greeting + starter pills, morphing voice/send/stop composer with attachments/project scope/web-search/canvas/memory toggles + effort slider, incognito private mode with dashed chrome and an isolated in-memory store, streaming transcript with auto-open reasoning disclosure, activity timeline, sources, stop/regenerate/edit-and-resend/Continue-on-length, thumbs feedback, token/cost line), sandboxed artifact cards (Preview/Code tabs, strict navigation policy), learning blocks (card/timeline/comparison/quiz/deep-dive), a 3-pane model selector (provider rail → grouped list → live metric detail pane with thinking slider), a trust-centered Memory page (sectioned summary, NL edits with undo ledger, suppressions, backfill, pause, export, 2-step reset), 7-pane Settings (account, plan-usage meters, appearance, personalization, chat defaults, memory, code, Advanced/BYOK), Projects (instructions+files, create-on-first-send), a v1 Cowork board, the engine-driven Code workbench (workspace sidebar, quick actions, permission modes, approval cards, Changes/Diff/Preview/Terminal/Tests/Git/Context inspector with checkpoints and dev-server preview), and dual voice surfaces (relay realtime orb UI; on-device Apple STT/TTS fallback). Apple-specific items flagged: Computer Use (ScreenCaptureKit/AX), on-device dictation/TTS, security-scoped bookmarks, Finder/Terminal integrations, iOS remote-code client; everything else is parity for the Tauri 2 + React client.
